function.name: Read
function.arguments: {"file_path": "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/CareOps/TASK-53/repo/API_tests/test_billing.sh"}

content.text:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# API tests: Billing Engine — Charges, Invoices, Payments, Refunds, Reconciliation
3	# Requires the full stack running via docker-compose
4	# Depends on data seeded by test_catalog_delivery.sh (services, packages, plan, delivery entries)
5	
6	set -euo pipefail
7	
8	BASE_URL="${BACKEND_URL:-http://localhost:8000}"
9	PASSED=0
10	FAILED=0
11	
12	check() {
13	    local name="$1"
14	    local expected="$2"
15	    local actual="$3"
16	    if [ "$actual" = "$expected" ]; then
17	        echo "  PASS  $name (got $actual)"
18	        PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
19	    else
20	        echo "  FAIL  $name (expected $expected, got $actual)"
21	        FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
22	    fi
23	}
24	
25	echo "======================================="
26	echo " Billing Engine API Tests"
27	echo "======================================="
28	echo "Target: $BASE_URL"
29	echo ""
30	
31	# --- Login ---
32	ADMIN_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
33	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
34	    -d '{"username":"admin","password":"Admin123!"}' 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
35	ADMIN_TOKEN=$(echo "$ADMIN_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['token'])" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
36	
37	BILLING_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
38	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
39	    -d '{"username":"billing_staff","password":"Billing123!"}' 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
40	BILLING_TOKEN=$(echo "$BILLING_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['token'])" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
41	
42	COACH_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
43	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
44	    -d '{"username":"coach","password":"Coach123!"}' 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
45	COACH_TOKEN=$(echo "$COACH_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['token'])" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
46	
47	AUDITOR_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
48	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
49	    -d '{"username":"auditor","password":"Auditor123!"}' 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
50	AUDITOR_TOKEN=$(echo "$AUDITOR_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['token'])" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
51	
52	if [ -z "$ADMIN_TOKEN" ]; then
53	    echo "FATAL: Could not get admin token. Backend may not be running."
54	    exit 1
55	fi
56	
57	# --- [1] Setup: seed a service, package, plan, and a verified delivery entry ---
58	echo "[Setup] Creating test service catalog item..."
59	SVC_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/catalog/" \
60	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
61	    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
62	    -d '{"code":"SVC-BILL-001","name":"Billing Test Nursing","category":"nursing","unit_type":"visit","default_rate":80.00}' \
63	    2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
64	BILL_SVC_ID=$(echo "$SVC_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
65	
66	echo "[Setup] Creating test package..."
67	PKG_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/packages/" \
68	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
69	    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
70	    -d "{\"code\":\"PKG-BILL\",\"name\":\"Billing Test Package\",\"rules\":[{\"service_item_id\":\"$BILL_SVC_ID\",\"rule_type\":\"per_visit\",\"rate\":80.00}]}" \
71	    2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
72	BILL_PKG_ID=$(echo "$PKG_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('package',{}).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
73	
74	echo "[Setup] Creating test plan..."
75	PLAN_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/plans/" \
76	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
77	    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
78	    -d '{"client_name":"Billing Test Client","start_date":"2024-01-01","end_date":"2024-12-31"}' \
79	    2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
80	BILL_PLAN_ID=$(echo "$PLAN_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
81	
82	# Activate the plan
83	curl -sf -X PUT "$BASE_URL/api/plans/$BILL_PLAN_ID" \
84	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
85	    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
86	    -d '{"status":"active"}' 2>/dev/null > /dev/null
87	
88	# Assign package to plan
89	ASSIGN_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/plans/$BILL_PLAN_ID/packages" \
90	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
91	    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
92	    -d "{\"package_id\":\"$BILL_PKG_ID\",\"effective_date\":\"2024-01-01\"}" \
93	    2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
94	BILL_PP_ID=$(echo "$ASSIGN_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
95	
96	# Create delivery entry (as coach)
97	ENTRY_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/delivery/" \
98	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
99	    -H "Authorization: Bearer $COACH_TOKEN" \
100	    -d "{\"plan_id\":\"$BILL_PLAN_ID\",\"plan_package_id\":\"$BILL_PP_ID\",\"service_item_id\":\"$BILL_SVC_ID\",\"delivery_date\":\"2024-02-10\",\"units\":1.0}" \
101	    2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
102	ENTRY_ID=$(echo "$ENTRY_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
103	
104	# Verify the delivery entry (admin has action.delivery.verify)
105	curl -sf -X PUT "$BASE_URL/api/delivery/$ENTRY_ID" \
106	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
107	    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
108	    -d '{"status":"verified"}' 2>/dev/null > /dev/null
109	
110	echo ""
111	echo "[1] Unauthenticated access to billing is rejected"
112	STATUS=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "$BASE_URL/api/billing/charges" 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
113	check "No auth -> 401" "401" "$STATUS"
114	
115	echo "[2] Coach cannot generate charges (missing action.billing.generate)"
116	STATUS=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/billing/charges/generate" \
117	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
118	    -H "Authorization: Bearer $COACH_TOKEN" \
119	    -d "{\"plan_id\":\"$BILL_PLAN_ID\"}" 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
120	check "Coach cannot generate charges -> 403" "403" "$STATUS"
121	
122	echo "[3] Admin generates charges from verified delivery entries"
123	CHARGE_RESP=""
124	CHARGE_ID=""
125	if [ -n "$BILL_PLAN_ID" ]; then
126	    CHARGE_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/billing/charges/generate" \
127	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
128	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
129	        -d "{\"plan_id\":\"$BILL_PLAN_ID\",\"from_date\":\"2024-01-01\",\"to_date\":\"2024-12-31\"}" \
130	        2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
131	    GENERATED=$(echo "$CHARGE_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('generated',0))" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
132	    check "Charge generation succeeds (>= 1 generated)" "true" "$([ "$GENERATED" -ge 1 ] && echo 'true' || echo 'false')"
133	    CHARGE_ID=$(echo "$CHARGE_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d['charges'][0]['id'] if d.get('charges') else '')" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
134	else
135	    echo "  SKIP (missing plan ID from setup)"
136	fi
137	
138	echo "[4] Regenerating charges for same plan skips already-charged entries"
139	if [ -n "$BILL_PLAN_ID" ]; then
140	    SKIP_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/billing/charges/generate" \
141	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
142	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
143	        -d "{\"plan_id\":\"$BILL_PLAN_ID\"}" \
144	        2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
145	    GENERATED2=$(echo "$SKIP_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('generated',1))" 2>/dev/null || echo "1")
146	    SKIPPED2=$(echo "$SKIP_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('skipped',0))" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
147	    check "Re-run generates 0 new charges" "0" "$GENERATED2"
148	else
149	    echo "  SKIP"
150	fi
151	
152	echo "[5] List charges returns 200"
153	STATUS=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
154	    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
155	    "$BASE_URL/api/billing/charges" 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
156	check "List charges -> 200" "200" "$STATUS"
157	
158	echo "[6] Get charge detail"
159	if [ -n "$CHARGE_ID" ]; then
160	    STATUS=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
161	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
162	        "$BASE_URL/api/billing/charges/$CHARGE_ID" 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
163	    check "Get charge detail -> 200" "200" "$STATUS"
164	else
165	    echo "  SKIP (no charge_id)"
166	fi
167	
168	echo "[7] Post adjustment to charge"
169	ADJ_ID=""
170	if [ -n "$CHARGE_ID" ]; then
171	    ADJ_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/billing/charges/$CHARGE_ID/adjustments" \
172	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
173	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
174	        -d '{"amount":-5.00,"reason":"Test rate correction"}' \
175	        2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
176	    ADJ_ID=$(echo "$ADJ_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
177	    check "Adjustment created" "true" "$([ -n "$ADJ_ID" ] && echo 'true' || echo 'false')"
178	else
179	    echo "  SKIP"
180	fi
181	
182	echo "[8] Zero-amount adjustment rejected"
183	if [ -n "$CHARGE_ID" ]; then
184	    STATUS=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/billing/charges/$CHARGE_ID/adjustments" \
185	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
186	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
187	        -d '{"amount":0.00,"reason":"Zero amount"}' \
188	        2>/dev/null || echo "000")
189	    check "Zero adjustment -> 400" "400" "$STATUS"
190	else
191	    echo "  SKIP"
192	fi
193	
194	echo "[9] Generate invoice from pending charges"
195	INVOICE_ID=""
196	if [ -n "$BILL_PLAN_ID" ]; then
197	    INV_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/billing/invoices/generate" \
198	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
199	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
200	        -d "{\"plan_id\":\"$BILL_PLAN_ID\",\"billing_period_start\":\"2024-01-01\",\"billing_period_end\":\"2024-12-31\"}" \
201	        2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
202	    INVOICE_ID=$(echo "$INV_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('invoice',{}).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
203	    check "Invoice generated" "true" "$([ -n "$INVOICE_ID" ] && echo 'true' || echo 'false')"
204	else
205	    echo "  SKIP"
206	fi
207	
208	echo "[10] Invoice with no pending charges returns 400"
209	if [ -n "$BILL_PLAN_ID" ]; then
210	    STATUS=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/billing/invoices/generate" \
211	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
212	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
213	        -d "{\"plan_id\":\"$BILL_PLAN_ID\",\"billing_period_start\":\"2024-01-01\",\"billing_period_end\":\"2024-12-31\"}" \
214	        2>/dev/null || echo "000")
215	    check "No pending charges -> 400" "400" "$STATUS"
216	else
217	    echo "  SKIP"
218	fi
219	
220	echo "[11] List invoices"
221	STATUS=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
222	    -H "Authorization: Bearer $BILLING_TOKEN" \
223	    "$BASE_URL/api/billing/invoices" 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
224	check "List invoices (billing staff) -> 200" "200" "$STATUS"
225	
226	echo "[12] Issue the invoice"
227	if [ -n "$INVOICE_ID" ]; then
228	    STATUS=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X PUT "$BASE_URL/api/billing/invoices/$INVOICE_ID/status" \
229	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
230	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
231	        -d '{"status":"issued"}' 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
232	    check "Issue invoice -> 200" "200" "$STATUS"
233	else
234	    echo "  SKIP"
235	fi
236	
237	echo "[13] Invalid status transition rejected"
238	if [ -n "$INVOICE_ID" ]; then
239	    STATUS=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X PUT "$BASE_URL/api/billing/invoices/$INVOICE_ID/status" \
240	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
241	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
242	        -d '{"status":"draft"}' 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
243	    check "issued->draft -> 400" "400" "$STATUS"
244	else
245	    echo "  SKIP"
246	fi
247	
248	echo "[14] List refund reason codes"
249	STATUS=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
250	    -H "Authorization: Bearer $BILLING_TOKEN" \
251	    "$BASE_URL/api/payments/reason-codes" 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
252	check "List reason codes -> 200" "200" "$STATUS"
253	
254	echo "[15] Record payment (with idempotency key)"
255	PAYMENT_ID=""
256	if [ -n "$INVOICE_ID" ]; then
257	    IDEM_KEY="test-payment-$(date +%s)"
258	    PAY_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/payments/" \
259	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
260	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $BILLING_TOKEN" \
261	        -d "{\"invoice_id\":\"$INVOICE_ID\",\"idempotency_key\":\"$IDEM_KEY\",\"payment_method\":\"check\",\"amount\":75.00,\"payment_date\":\"2024-03-01\"}" \
262	        2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
263	    PAYMENT_ID=$(echo "$PAY_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
264	    check "Payment recorded" "true" "$([ -n "$PAYMENT_ID" ] && echo 'true' || echo 'false')"
265	else
266	    echo "  SKIP"
267	fi
268	
269	echo "[16] Duplicate idempotency key within 5 min -> 409 Conflict"
270	if [ -n "$INVOICE_ID" ] && [ -n "$IDEM_KEY" ]; then
271	    STATUS=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/payments/" \
272	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
273	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $BILLING_TOKEN" \
274	        -d "{\"invoice_id\":\"$INVOICE_ID\",\"idempotency_key\":\"$IDEM_KEY\",\"payment_method\":\"check\",\"amount\":75.00,\"payment_date\":\"2024-03-01\"}" \
275	        2>/dev/null || echo "000")
276	    check "Duplicate key within 5 min -> 409" "409" "$STATUS"
277	else
278	    echo "  SKIP"
279	fi
280	
281	echo "[17] Different idempotency key accepted"
282	PAYMENT_ID2=""
283	if [ -n "$INVOICE_ID" ]; then
284	    IDEM_KEY2="test-payment-$(date +%s)-b"
285	    PAY_RESP2=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/payments/" \
286	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
287	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $BILLING_TOKEN" \
288	        -d "{\"invoice_id\":\"$INVOICE_ID\",\"idempotency_key\":\"$IDEM_KEY2\",\"payment_method\":\"ach\",\"amount\":4.00,\"payment_date\":\"2024-03-02\"}" \
289	        2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
290	    PAYMENT_ID2=$(echo "$PAY_RESP2" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
291	    check "Second payment with different key accepted" "true" "$([ -n "$PAYMENT_ID2" ] && echo 'true' || echo 'false')"
292	else
293	    echo "  SKIP"
294	fi
295	
296	echo "[18] Refund within net-paid amount succeeds"
297	REFUND_ID=""
298	if [ -n "$INVOICE_ID" ]; then
299	    REF_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/payments/refunds" \
300	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
301	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $BILLING_TOKEN" \
302	        -d "{\"invoice_id\":\"$INVOICE_ID\",\"reason_code\":\"BILLING_ERROR\",\"amount\":5.00,\"refund_method\":\"check\",\"refund_date\":\"2024-03-05\",\"reason_notes\":\"Test refund\"}" \
303	        2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
304	    REFUND_ID=$(echo "$REF_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
305	    check "Refund within net-paid succeeds" "true" "$([ -n "$REFUND_ID" ] && echo 'true' || echo 'false')"
306	else
307	    echo "  SKIP"
308	fi
309	
310	echo "[19] Refund exceeding net-paid amount rejected"
311	if [ -n "$INVOICE_ID" ]; then
312	    STATUS=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/payments/refunds" \
313	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
314	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $BILLING_TOKEN" \
315	        -d "{\"invoice_id\":\"$INVOICE_ID\",\"reason_code\":\"BILLING_ERROR\",\"amount\":9999.00,\"refund_method\":\"check\",\"refund_date\":\"2024-03-06\"}" \
316	        2>/dev/null || echo "000")
317	    check "Over-cap refund -> 400" "400" "$STATUS"
318	else
319	    echo "  SKIP"
320	fi
321	
322	echo "[20] Refund with invalid reason code rejected"
323	if [ -n "$INVOICE_ID" ]; then
324	    STATUS=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/payments/refunds" \
325	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
326	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $BILLING_TOKEN" \
327	        -d "{\"invoice_id\":\"$INVOICE_ID\",\"reason_code\":\"MADE_UP_CODE\",\"amount\":1.00,\"refund_method\":\"check\",\"refund_date\":\"2024-03-07\"}" \
328	        2>/dev/null || echo "000")
329	    check "Invalid reason code -> 400" "400" "$STATUS"
330	else
331	    echo "  SKIP"
332	fi
333	
334	echo "[21] Coach cannot record payments (403)"
335	if [ -n "$INVOICE_ID" ]; then
336	    STATUS=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/payments/" \
337	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
338	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $COACH_TOKEN" \
339	        -d "{\"invoice_id\":\"$INVOICE_ID\",\"idempotency_key\":\"coach-test\",\"payment_method\":\"cash\",\"amount\":1.00,\"payment_date\":\"2024-03-01\"}" \
340	        2>/dev/null || echo "000")
341	    check "Coach cannot record payment -> 403" "403" "$STATUS"
342	else
343	    echo "  SKIP"
344	fi
345	
346	echo "[22] List fund transactions (immutable ledger)"
347	STATUS=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
348	    -H "Authorization: Bearer $BILLING_TOKEN" \
349	    "$BASE_URL/api/payments/transactions" 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
350	check "List fund transactions -> 200" "200" "$STATUS"
351	
352	echo "[23] Generate reconciliation run"
353	RECON_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/payments/reconciliation" \
354	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
355	    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
356	    -d '{"period_start":"2024-01-01","period_end":"2024-12-31"}' \
357	    2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
358	RECON_ID=$(echo "$RECON_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
359	check "Reconciliation generated" "true" "$([ -n "$RECON_ID" ] && echo 'true' || echo 'false')"
360	
361	echo "[24] Invalid period rejected for reconciliation"
362	STATUS=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/payments/reconciliation" \
363	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
364	    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
365	    -d '{"period_start":"2024-12-31","period_end":"2024-01-01"}' \
366	    2>/dev/null || echo "000")
367	check "period_end < period_start -> 400" "400" "$STATUS"
368	
369	echo "[25] Auditor cannot post adjustments (403)"
370	if [ -n "$CHARGE_ID" ]; then
371	    STATUS=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/billing/charges/$CHARGE_ID/adjustments" \
372	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
373	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $AUDITOR_TOKEN" \
374	        -d '{"amount":-1.00,"reason":"Auditor test"}' \
375	        2>/dev/null || echo "000")
376	    check "Auditor cannot post adjustment -> 403" "403" "$STATUS"
377	else
378	    echo "  SKIP"
379	fi
380	
381	echo "[26] Duplicate idempotency key (DB constraint) -> 409"
382	if [ -n "$INVOICE_ID" ]; then
383	    ATOMIC_KEY="atomic-idem-$(date +%s)"
384	    # First insert should succeed
385	    FIRST=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/payments/" \
386	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
387	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $BILLING_TOKEN" \
388	        -d "{\"invoice_id\":\"$INVOICE_ID\",\"idempotency_key\":\"$ATOMIC_KEY\",\"payment_method\":\"cash\",\"amount\":0.01,\"payment_date\":\"2024-04-01\"}" \
389	        2>/dev/null || echo "000")
390	    # Second insert with same key should hit unique constraint -> 409
391	    SECOND=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/payments/" \
392	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
393	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $BILLING_TOKEN" \
394	        -d "{\"invoice_id\":\"$INVOICE_ID\",\"idempotency_key\":\"$ATOMIC_KEY\",\"payment_method\":\"cash\",\"amount\":0.01,\"payment_date\":\"2024-04-01\"}" \
395	        2>/dev/null || echo "000")
396	    check "Atomic idempotency: second insert -> 409" "409" "$SECOND"
397	else
398	    echo "  SKIP"
399	fi
400	
401	# ============================================================
402	# Data-scope enforcement tests (O-03)
403	#
404	# These tests create a "scopeless" user that carries the Billing
405	# Specialist role (and therefore the api.payments.* permissions)
406	# but has NO row in user_data_scopes.  Every payments/refunds
407	# endpoint should deny this user with 403, proving that the
408	# require_data_scope guard is active and cannot be bypassed by
409	# having the permission alone.
410	# ============================================================
411	
412	echo ""
413	echo "--- Data-scope enforcement (payments/refunds routes) ---"
414	
415	# Step 1: look up the org_id used by the demo environment
416	ORG_ID=$(curl -sf -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" "$BASE_URL/api/admin/org/" 2>/dev/null \
417	    | python3 -c "import sys,json; orgs=json.load(sys.stdin); print(orgs[0]['id'] if orgs else '')" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
418	
419	# Step 2: look up the "Billing Specialist" role ID
420	BILLING_ROLE_ID=$(curl -sf -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" "$BASE_URL/api/roles/" 2>/dev/null \
421	    | python3 -c "
422	import sys,json
423	roles=json.load(sys.stdin)
424	for r in roles:
425	    if r['name']=='Billing Specialist':
426	        print(r['id']); break
427	" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
428	
429	SCOPELESS_TOKEN=""
430	if [ -n "$ORG_ID" ] && [ -n "$BILLING_ROLE_ID" ]; then
431	    # Step 3: create a user that will intentionally have NO data scope
432	    SCOPELESS_USER="scopeless_billing_$(date +%s)"
433	    CREATE_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/users/" \
434	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
435	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
436	        -d "{\"username\":\"$SCOPELESS_USER\",\"email\":\"${SCOPELESS_USER}@test.local\",\"password\":\"Sc0peless!\",\"org_id\":\"$ORG_ID\"}" \
437	        2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
438	    SCOPELESS_UID=$(echo "$CREATE_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
439	
440	    if [ -n "$SCOPELESS_UID" ]; then
441	        # Step 4: assign Billing Specialist role (gives api.payments.* permissions)
442	        curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/users/$SCOPELESS_UID/roles" \
443	            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
444	            -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
445	            -d "{\"role_id\":\"$BILLING_ROLE_ID\"}" 2>/dev/null > /dev/null
446	
447	        # NOTE: we intentionally do NOT assign any data scope.
448	        # The user has the permission but not the scope.
449	
450	        # Step 5: log in as the scopeless user
451	        SCOPELESS_LOGIN=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
452	            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
453	            -d "{\"username\":\"$SCOPELESS_USER\",\"password\":\"Sc0peless!\"}" 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
454	        SCOPELESS_TOKEN=$(echo "$SCOPELESS_LOGIN" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['token'])" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
455	    fi
456	fi
457	
458	if [ -n "$SCOPELESS_TOKEN" ]; then
459	    echo "[27] Scopeless user cannot list payments (has permission, no scope)"
460	    STATUS=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
461	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $SCOPELESS_TOKEN" \
462	        "$BASE_URL/api/payments/?limit=5" 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
463	    check "Scopeless list payments -> 403" "403" "$STATUS"
464	
465	    echo "[28] Scopeless user cannot record payment (has permission, no scope)"
466	    STATUS=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/payments/" \
467	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
468	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $SCOPELESS_TOKEN" \
469	        -d '{"invoice_id":"fake","idempotency_key":"scope-test","payment_method":"cash","amount":1.00,"payment_date":"2024-01-01"}' \
470	        2>/dev/null || echo "000")
471	    check "Scopeless record payment -> 403" "403" "$STATUS"
472	
473	    echo "[29] Scopeless user cannot list refunds (has permission, no scope)"
474	    STATUS=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
475	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $SCOPELESS_TOKEN" \
476	        "$BASE_URL/api/payments/refunds?limit=5" 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
477	    check "Scopeless list refunds -> 403" "403" "$STATUS"
478	
479	    echo "[30] Scopeless user cannot record refund (has permission, no scope)"
480	    STATUS=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/payments/refunds" \
481	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
482	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $SCOPELESS_TOKEN" \
483	        -d '{"invoice_id":"fake","reason_code":"OTHER","amount":1.00,"refund_method":"cash","refund_date":"2024-01-01"}' \
484	        2>/dev/null || echo "000")
485	    check "Scopeless record refund -> 403" "403" "$STATUS"
486	
487	    echo "[31] Scopeless user cannot list fund transactions (has permission, no scope)"
488	    STATUS=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
489	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $SCOPELESS_TOKEN" \
490	        "$BASE_URL/api/payments/transactions?limit=5" 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
491	    check "Scopeless list fund txns -> 403" "403" "$STATUS"
492	
493	    echo "[32] Scopeless user cannot list reason codes (has permission, no scope)"
494	    STATUS=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
495	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $SCOPELESS_TOKEN" \
496	        "$BASE_URL/api/payments/reason-codes" 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
497	    check "Scopeless list reason codes -> 403" "403" "$STATUS"
498	
499	    echo "[33] Scopeless user cannot generate reconciliation (has permission, no scope)"
500	    STATUS=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/payments/reconciliation" \
501	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
502	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $SCOPELESS_TOKEN" \
503	        -d '{"period_start":"2024-01-01","period_end":"2024-12-31"}' \
504	        2>/dev/null || echo "000")
505	    check "Scopeless generate recon -> 403" "403" "$STATUS"
506	
507	    echo "[34] Scopeless user cannot list reconciliation runs (has permission, no scope)"
508	    STATUS=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
509	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $SCOPELESS_TOKEN" \
510	        "$BASE_URL/api/payments/reconciliation?limit=5" 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
511	    check "Scopeless list recon -> 403" "403" "$STATUS"
512	else
513	    echo "  SKIP [27-34] (could not create scopeless test user; needs admin API)"
514	fi
515	
516	echo ""
517	echo "======================================="
518	echo "Results: $PASSED passed, $FAILED failed"
519	echo "======================================="
520	[ "$FAILED" -eq 0 ] || exit 1
521

content.text:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# API tests: Reports & Exports
3	# Tests: order volume, revenue, utilization, KPI endpoints with date filters,
4	#        export masking defaults, unmasked export gating, unknown export type 400.
5	
6	set -euo pipefail
7	
8	BASE_URL="${BACKEND_URL:-http://localhost:8000}"
9	PASSED=0
10	FAILED=0
11	
12	check() {
13	    local name="$1"
14	    local expected="$2"
15	    local actual="$3"
16	    if [ "$actual" = "$expected" ]; then
17	        echo "  PASS  $name (got $actual)"
18	        PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
19	    else
20	        echo "  FAIL  $name (expected $expected, got $actual)"
21	        FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
22	    fi
23	}
24	
25	check_contains() {
26	    local name="$1"
27	    local needle="$2"
28	    local haystack="$3"
29	    if echo "$haystack" | grep -q "$needle"; then
30	        echo "  PASS  $name (contains '$needle')"
31	        PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
32	    else
33	        echo "  FAIL  $name (expected to contain '$needle')"
34	        echo "        Got: $haystack" | head -c 300
35	        FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
36	    fi
37	}
38	
39	check_not_contains() {
40	    local name="$1"
41	    local needle="$2"
42	    local haystack="$3"
43	    if echo "$haystack" | grep -q "$needle"; then
44	        echo "  FAIL  $name (should NOT contain '$needle')"
45	        FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
46	    else
47	        echo "  PASS  $name (does not contain '$needle')"
48	        PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
49	    fi
50	}
51	
52	echo "======================================="
53	echo " Reports & Exports API Tests"
54	echo "======================================="
55	echo "Target: $BASE_URL"
56	echo ""
57	
58	# =============================================================
59	# Auth
60	# =============================================================
61	
62	ADMIN_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
63	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
64	    -d '{"username":"admin","password":"Admin123!"}' 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
65	ADMIN_TOKEN=$(echo "$ADMIN_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['token'])" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
66	
67	AUDITOR_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
68	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
69	    -d '{"username":"auditor","password":"Auditor123!"}' 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
70	AUDITOR_TOKEN=$(echo "$AUDITOR_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['token'])" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
71	
72	COACH_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
73	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
74	    -d '{"username":"coach","password":"Coach123!"}' 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
75	COACH_TOKEN=$(echo "$COACH_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['token'])" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
76	
77	echo "[1] Auth setup"
78	check "Admin token obtained" "1" "$([ -n "$ADMIN_TOKEN" ] && echo 1 || echo 0)"
79	
80	# =============================================================
81	# Unauthenticated
82	# =============================================================
83	
84	echo ""
85	echo "[2] Unauthenticated access"
86	
87	STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
88	    "$BASE_URL/api/reports/kpi?from_date=2024-01-01&to_date=2024-12-31")
89	check "KPI — no token → 401" "401" "$STATUS"
90	
91	# =============================================================
92	# Coach lacks reports permission
93	# =============================================================
94	
95	echo ""
96	echo "[3] Coach cannot access reports"
97	
98	STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \

162	
163	EXPORT_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/reports/export" \
164	    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
165	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
166	    -d '{

171

173	check_contains "Export has export_log_id" "export_log_id" "$EXPORT_RESP"
174	check_contains "Export has row_count" "row_count" "$EXPORT_RESP"
175	
176	# Client names should be masked
177	if echo "$EXPORT_RESP" | python3 -c "
178	import sys, json
179	d = json.load(sys.stdin)
180	rows = d.get('rows', [])
181	if not rows:
182	    print('no_rows')
183	    sys.exit(0)
184	# All client_name fields must be ****
185	bad = [r for r in rows if r.get('client_name') != '****']
186	print('ok' if not bad else 'has_unmasked')
187	" 2>/dev/null | grep -q "ok\|no_rows"; then
188	    echo "  PASS  Masked export — client_name is '****' or no rows"
189	    PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
190	else
191	    echo "  FAIL  Masked export — client_name not masked"
192	    FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
193	fi
194	
195	# =============================================================

197	# =============================================================
198

205	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \

207	check "Unknown export_type → 400" "400" "$STATUS"
208	
209	STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \

211	    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
212	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
213	    -d '{"export_type":"deliveries","from_date":"not-a-date","to_date":"2024-12-31"}')
214	check "Invalid from_date format → 400" "400" "$STATUS"
215	
216	# =============================================================
217	# Coach cannot export
218	# =============================================================
219

221	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/reports/export" \
222	    -H "Authorization: Bearer $COACH_TOKEN" \
223	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
224	    -d '{"export_type":"deliveries","from_date":"2024-01-01","to_date":"2024-12-31"}')
225	check "Coach export → 403" "403" "$STATUS"

235	    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \

238	        "export_type": "deliveries",

240	        "to_date": "2024-12-31",
241	        "unmasked": true
242	    }' 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
243	
244	check_contains "Unmasked export — admin gets masked=false" "\"masked\":false" "$UNMASKED_RESP"

256	    -d '{
257	        "export_type": "evaluations",
258	        "from_date": "2024-01-01",
259	        "to_date": "2024-12-31"
260	    }' 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
261	check_contains "Evaluations export has row_count" "row_count" "$EVAL_EXPORT"
262	check_contains "Evaluations export has masked=true" "\"masked\":true" "$EVAL_EXPORT"
263	
264	# =============================================================
265	# Export — project_id filtering (backward compat + new field)
266	# =============================================================
267	
268	echo ""
269	echo "[12] Export with department_id only (backward compat)"
270	
271	DEPT_EXPORT=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/reports/export" \
272	    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
273	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
274	    -d '{
275	        "export_type": "deliveries",
276	        "from_date": "2024-01-01",
277	        "to_date": "2024-12-31",
278	        "department_id": "nonexistent-dept-id"
279	    }' 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
280	# Should succeed (200) with 0 rows — the department doesn't exist
281	check_contains "Dept-only export has row_count" "row_count" "$DEPT_EXPORT"

284	echo "[13] Export with department_id + project_id"
285	
286	PROJ_EXPORT=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/reports/export" \
287	    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
288	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
289	    -d '{
290	        "export_type": "deliveries",
291	        "from_date": "2024-01-01",
292	        "to_date": "2024-12-31",
293	        "department_id": "nonexistent-dept",
294	        "project_id": "nonexistent-proj"
295	    }' 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
296	check_contains "Dept+project export has row_count" "row_count" "$PROJ_EXPORT"
297

302	    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \

304	    -d '{

321	        "from_date": "2024-01-01",
322	        "to_date": "2024-12-31"
323	    }' 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
324	check_contains "No project_id field — still returns row_count" "row_count" "$COMPAT_EXPORT"
325	
326	# =============================================================
327	# Cross-project scope 403 — user scoped to project_A cannot
328	# export data filtered to project_B
329	# =============================================================
330	
331	echo ""
332	echo "[16] Cross-project scope enforcement"
333	
334	# Discover the demo org and create a project to scope a test user to
335	ORG_ID=$(curl -sf -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" "$BASE_URL/api/admin/org/" 2>/dev/null \
336	    | python3 -c "import sys,json; orgs=json.load(sys.stdin); print(orgs[0]['id'] if orgs else '')" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
337	
338	# Find a department id to anchor the project

341	    | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d[0]['id'] if d else '')" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
342	
343	# Find the Billing Specialist role (has action.reports.export)
344	BILLING_ROLE_ID=$(curl -sf -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" "$BASE_URL/api/roles/" 2>/dev/null \
345	    | python3 -c "
346	import sys,json
347	roles=json.load(sys.stdin)
348	for r in roles:
349	    if r['name']=='Billing Specialist':
350	        print(r['id']); break
351	" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
352	
353	SCOPE_TOKEN=""
354	if [ -n "$ORG_ID" ] && [ -n "$DEPT_ID" ] && [ -n "$BILLING_ROLE_ID" ]; then
355	    # Create two projects under the department
356	    PROJ_A_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/admin/org/$ORG_ID/departments/$DEPT_ID/projects" \
357	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
358	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
359	        -d '{"name":"ScopeTest Project A"}' 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
360	    PROJ_A_ID=$(echo "$PROJ_A_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
361	
362	    PROJ_B_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/admin/org/$ORG_ID/departments/$DEPT_ID/projects" \
363	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
364	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
365	        -d '{"name":"ScopeTest Project B"}' 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
366	    PROJ_B_ID=$(echo "$PROJ_B_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
367	
368	    # Create a test user scoped to Project A only
369	    SCOPE_USER="projscope_$(date +%s)"
370	    CREATE_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/users/" \
371	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
372	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
373	        -d "{\"username\":\"$SCOPE_USER\",\"email\":\"${SCOPE_USER}@test.local\",\"password\":\"Sc0pe123!\",\"org_id\":\"$ORG_ID\"}" \
374	        2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
375	    SCOPE_UID=$(echo "$CREATE_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
376	
377	    if [ -n "$SCOPE_UID" ] && [ -n "$PROJ_A_ID" ] && [ -n "$PROJ_B_ID" ]; then
378	        # Assign Billing Specialist role (gives action.reports.export)
379	        curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/users/$SCOPE_UID/roles" \
380	            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
381	            -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
382	            -d "{\"role_id\":\"$BILLING_ROLE_ID\"}" 2>/dev/null > /dev/null
383	
384	        # Grant scope to Project A only (not B)
385	        curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/users/$SCOPE_UID/scopes" \
386	            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
387	            -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
388	            -d "{\"org_id\":\"$ORG_ID\",\"department_id\":\"$DEPT_ID\",\"project_id\":\"$PROJ_A_ID\",\"access_level\":\"read\"}" \
389	            2>/dev/null > /dev/null
390	
391	        # Log in as the scoped user
392	        SCOPE_LOGIN=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
393	            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
394	            -d "{\"username\":\"$SCOPE_USER\",\"password\":\"Sc0pe123!\"}" 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
395	        SCOPE_TOKEN=$(echo "$SCOPE_LOGIN" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['token'])" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
396	    fi
397	fi
398	
399	if [ -n "$SCOPE_TOKEN" ] && [ -n "$PROJ_A_ID" ] && [ -n "$PROJ_B_ID" ] && [ -n "$DEPT_ID" ]; then
400	    # Export for Project A (user has scope) — should succeed
401	    STATUS_A=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
402	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/reports/export" \
403	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $SCOPE_TOKEN" \
404	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
405	        -d "{\"export_type\":\"deliveries\",\"from_date\":\"2024-01-01\",\"to_date\":\"2024-12-31\",\"department_id\":\"$DEPT_ID\",\"project_id\":\"$PROJ_A_ID\"}" \
406	        2>/dev/null || echo "000")
407	    check "Scoped user export own project -> 200" "200" "$STATUS_A"
408	
409	    # Export for Project B (user does NOT have scope) — should be 403
410	    STATUS_B=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
411	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/reports/export" \
412	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $SCOPE_TOKEN" \
413	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
414	        -d "{\"export_type\":\"deliveries\",\"from_date\":\"2024-01-01\",\"to_date\":\"2024-12-31\",\"department_id\":\"$DEPT_ID\",\"project_id\":\"$PROJ_B_ID\"}" \
415	        2>/dev/null || echo "000")
416	    check "Scoped user export OTHER project -> 403" "403" "$STATUS_B"
417	else
418	    echo "  SKIP [16] (could not create scoped test user/projects)"
419	fi
420	
421	# =============================================================
422	# Summary
423	# =============================================================
424	
425	echo ""
426	echo "======================================="
427	echo " Results: $PASSED passed, $FAILED failed"
428	echo "======================================="

430	[ "$FAILED" -eq 0 ] || exit 1

content.text:
1	# Billing & Financial Controls
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	The billing engine converts verified service delivery records into auditable financial transactions. The flow is strictly additive: every real money movement is recorded as an immutable `fund_transaction` entry. No billing record is ever deleted or overwritten — corrections are new records that reference the original.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## 1. Billing Lifecycle
10	
11	```
12	Delivery Entry (verified)
13	        │
14	        ▼
15	   Charge Generated  ──── Charge Adjustment (optional, additive)
16	        │
17	        ▼
18	   Invoice (draft) ──── Line Items (one per charge, immutable snapshot)
19	        │
20	        ▼
21	   Invoice (issued)
22	        │
23	        ├──── Recorded Payment ──── Fund Transaction (credit, immutable)
24	        │           │
25	        │           ▼
26	        │     Invoice → paid / partially_paid
27	        │
28	        └──── Recorded Refund ──── Fund Transaction (debit, immutable)
29	                    │
30	                    ▼
31	              Net paid recalculated
32	```
33	
34	### States
35	
36	**Charge statuses**
37	
38	| Status | Meaning |
39	|--------|---------|
40	| `pending` | Generated, not yet adjusted or invoiced |
41	| `adjusted` | Has one or more charge adjustments |
42	| `invoiced` | Included in an invoice; no further adjustments allowed |
43	| `voided` | Excluded from billing (manual void) |
44	
45	**Invoice statuses**
46	
47	| Status | Meaning | Allowed next states |
48	|--------|---------|---------------------|
49	| `draft` | Generated, not yet sent | `issued`, `voided` |
50	| `issued` | Sent to payer | `paid`, `partially_paid`, `voided` |
51	| `partially_paid` | Partial payment received | `paid`, `voided` |
52	| `paid` | Full net payment received | _(terminal)_ |
53	| `voided` | Cancelled | _(terminal)_ |
54	
55	---
56	
57	## 2. Charge Generation
58	
59	**Endpoint:** `POST /api/billing/charges/generate`
60

116:## 5. Idempotency Model

118-**Endpoint:** `POST /api/payments/`
119-
120:Every payment request must include an `idempotency_key` (caller-supplied, free-form string). The system enforces a **5-minute duplicate rejection window**:
121-
122:- If the same `idempotency_key` was used for the same organization within the last 5 minutes → **409 Conflict**
123:- After 5 minutes, the same key is accepted as a new (retry) payment — this handles network retries without double-charging
124-
125-**Best practice for callers:**
126-- Use a UUID generated at the time the user clicks "Record Payment"
127-- Retain the key for retry in case of network failure
128-
129:The key is not globally unique — it is scoped per organization and per 5-minute window. Two organizations may use the same key without conflict.

**Issue 2 - KPI**: In `report_service.rs`, the pattern `(pct_value * 100.0).round() / 100.0` where `pct_value` is already a percentage looks like double-multiplication. While mathematically it's just rounding to 2 decimal places, the code needs to be clarified with a proper rounding helper.

113	
114	---
115	
116	## 5. Idempotency Model
117	
118	**Endpoint:** `POST /api/payments/`
119	
120	Every payment request must include an `idempotency_key` (caller-supplied, free-form string). The system enforces a **5-minute duplicate rejection window**:
121	
122	- If the same `idempotency_key` was used for the same organization within the last 5 minutes → **409 Conflict**
123	- After 5 minutes, the same key is accepted as a new (retry) payment — this handles network retries without double-charging
124	
125	**Best practice for callers:**
126	- Use a UUID generated at the time the user clicks "Record Payment"
127	- Retain the key for retry in case of network failure
128	
129	The key is not globally unique — it is scoped per organization and per 5-minute window. Two organizations may use the same key without conflict.
130	
131	---
132

Good, docs already describe the correct 5-minute window behavior. Now let me implement all changes.

**Step 1: New migration to restore 5-minute idempotency window with race safety.**

content.name: Write
content.arguments:
{"file_path": "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/CareOps/TASK-53/repo/backend/migrations/20240108000000_restore_idempotency_window.sql", "content": "-- Migration: Restore 5-minute idempotency window for payments
--
-- The previous migration (20240107) replaced the time-windowed idempotency
-- check with a permanent UNIQUE constraint on (org_id, idempotency_key).
-- This broke the documented 5-minute window behavior: callers should be able
-- to reuse the same key after 5 minutes (network retry scenario).
--
-- This migration introduces a dedicated `payment_idempotency_keys` table
-- with a PRIMARY KEY on (org_id, idempotency_key).  The application uses
-- INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE with a 5-minute time check, which is
-- both race-safe (no SELECT-then-INSERT gap) and time-windowed.
--
-- The UNIQUE constraint on recorded_payments is dropped because the same
-- key can now appear in multiple payment records (after window expiry).

-- 1. Create the idempotency tracking table
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS payment_idempotency_keys (
    org_id CHAR(36) NOT NULL,
    idempotency_key VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    PRIMARY KEY (org_id, idempotency_key)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

-- 2. Seed from existing payments so in-flight keys are still protected
INSERT IGNORE INTO payment_idempotency_keys (org_id, idempotency_key, created_at)
SELECT org_id, idempotency_key, created_at FROM recorded_payments;

-- 3. Drop the permanent unique constraint from recorded_payments
ALTER TABLE recorded_payments DROP INDEX uq_payments_org_idempotency_key;

-- 4. Restore a non-unique index for lookup performance
CREATE INDEX idx_payment_idempotency ON recorded_payments (org_id, idempotency_key, created_at);
"}

# KPI numeric sanity: all percentage fields must be in the 0-100 range.
# If any value exceeds 100, the double-*100 bug has regressed.

KPI_RANGE_OK=$(echo "$KPI_RESP" | python3 -c "
import sys, json
try:
    d = json.load(sys.stdin)
    fields = ['attendance_rate_pct','repurchase_rate_pct','staff_utilization_pct','second_review_rate_pct']
    for f in fields:
        v = d.get(f, 0)
        if not (0 <= v <= 100):
            print(f'FAIL:{f}={v}')
            sys.exit(0)
    # avg_score is on 0-100 scale but may be null
    avg = d.get('avg_score')
    if avg is not None and not (0 <= avg <= 100):
        print(f'FAIL:avg_score={avg}')
        sys.exit(0)
    print('OK')
except Exception as e:
    print(f'ERROR:{e}')
" 2>/dev/null || echo "ERROR")

" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

check_contains "KPI response has repurchase_rate_pct" "repurchase_rate_pct" "$KPI_RESP"
check_contains "KPI response has staff_utilization_pct" "staff_utilization_pct" "$KPI_RESP"

check_contains "KPI has period_start" "period_start" "$KPI_RESP"

# KPI numeric sanity: all percentage fields must be in the 0-100 range.
# If any value exceeds 100, the double-*100 bug has regressed.
echo ""
echo "[4b] KPI numeric range assertions (percentages must be 0–100)"

KPI_RANGE_OK=$(echo "$KPI_RESP" | python3 -c "
import sys, json
try:
    d = json.load(sys.stdin)
    fields = ['attendance_rate_pct','repurchase_rate_pct','staff_utilization_pct','second_review_rate_pct']
    for f in fields:
        v = d.get(f, 0)
        if not (0 <= v <= 100):
            print(f'FAIL:{f}={v}')
            sys.exit(0)
    # avg_score is on 0-100 scale but may be null
    avg = d.get('avg_score')
    if avg is not None and not (0 <= avg <= 100):
        print(f'FAIL:avg_score={avg}')
        sys.exit(0)
    print('OK')
except Exception as e:
    print(f'ERROR:{e}')
" 2>/dev/null || echo "ERROR")

check "KPI pct fields all in 0–100 range" "OK" "$KPI_RANGE_OK"

# Verify period echoed correctly
KPI_PERIOD=$(echo "$KPI_RESP" | python3 -c "
import sys,json
d=json.load(sys.stdin)
print(f\"{d['period_start']}:{d['period_end']}\")
" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
check "KPI period matches request" "2024-01-01:2024-12-31" "$KPI_PERIOD""}

88	        // atomically handles both the "new key" and "expired key" paths in a
89	        // single round-trip, with no SELECT-then-INSERT race window.
90	        //
91	        // MySQL affected-rows semantics:

93	        //   2 = existing row was updated    → key expired, proceed
94	        //   0 = existing row unchanged      → key is active, reject 409

421	/// Round an f64 to two decimal places.  Used for KPI output formatting.
422	/// This is purely a rounding operation — it does NOT convert fractions to
423	/// percentages.  Values are expected to already be on their final scale.
424	fn round_2dp(v: f64) -> f64 {
425	    (v * 100.0).round() / 100.0

All consistent. Here's a summary of all changes:

---

## Summary of Changes

### Fix 1: Payment Idempotency — Restore 5-Minute Window

**Root Cause**: Migration `20240107` replaced the time-windowed idempotency approach with a permanent `UNIQUE (org_id, idempotency_key)` constraint. This made keys permanently unique per org, conflicting with the documented/required 5-minute duplicate rejection window.

**Files changed:**

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `backend/migrations/20240108000000_restore_idempotency_window.sql` | **New migration**: Creates `payment_idempotency_keys` table with `PRIMARY KEY (org_id, idempotency_key)`, seeds it from existing payments, drops the permanent UNIQUE constraint from `recorded_payments`, restores the original non-unique index. |
| `backend/src/application/payment_service.rs` | Replaced INSERT-and-catch-duplicate with `INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE` against the new idempotency table. Uses MySQL's affected-rows semantics: 0=active key within 5 min (409), 1=new key (proceed), 2=expired key refreshed (proceed). Race-safe and time-windowed in a single atomic statement. |
| `API_tests/test_billing.sh` | Added first-insert assertion (200) to test [26]; added test [26b] documenting the window-expiry path. |
| `docs/billing_and_financial_controls.md` | Added implementation note describing the `INSERT ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE` strategy. |
| `docs/requirements_traceability.md` | Updated idempotency row to reference new migration and mechanism. |

### Fix 2: KPI Percentage Output Clarity

**Root Cause**: The pattern `(pct_value * 100.0).round() / 100.0` where `pct_value` is already on the 0–100 percentage scale looks like it multiplies by 100 twice. While mathematically this is just rounding to 2 decimal places (correct), the code was confusing and flagged as double-multiplication.

**Files changed:**

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `backend/src/application/report_service.rs` | Extracted `round_2dp()` helper function with clear doc comment. Replaced all 6 instances of `(x * 100.0).round() / 100.0` with `round_2dp(x)` — in `kpi_summary` (4 KPI fields + `avg_score`) and in `revenue_report` (`refund_rate_pct`). Same mathematical result, unambiguous intent. |
| `API_tests/test_reports.sh` | Added test [4b]: numeric range assertion ensuring all KPI `_pct` fields are in 0–100 range (catches double-*100 regression), plus period echo verification. |
| `docs/requirements_traceability.md` | Updated KPI row to reference `round_2dp` helper and new test. |

### Backward Compatibility

- No API endpoints, request/response schemas, or status codes changed
- KPI field names unchanged; values are mathematically identical (same rounding, just clearer code)
- Payment 409 error envelope shape preserved; error message now mentions "within the last 5 minutes"
- New migration is additive (new table) + safe ALTER (drop unique, add index)

You are a senior Rust full-stack engineer working in this repository.

Issue to fix (BLOCKER):
- `backend/src/application/export_service.rs` uses `JOIN service_items si ON si.id = de.service_item_id`
- Actual schema defines `service_catalog_items` (see migration `backend/migrations/20240103000000_catalog_packages_plans_delivery.sql`).
- This likely breaks `export_type = deliveries`.

Goal:
- Fix delivery export query so it matches the real schema.
- Do this without breaking existing API contracts or response shape.

Constraints:
- No breaking changes to API routes or JSON fields.
- Keep export behavior backward-compatible.
- Keep masking logic unchanged.
- Keep existing filters (`department_id`, `project_id`) behavior unchanged.

Tasks:
1) Update the SQL join in `export_service.rs` to the correct table name.
2) Verify all referenced columns still exist with same semantics.
3) Add/extend tests (unit/integration) so this mismatch cannot regress:
   - successful deliveries export path
   - non-empty result fixture (or deterministic minimal seeded row)
   - assertion that export returns 200-equivalent success response and expected fields
4) If any docs mention old naming, align them.

Deliverables:
- Code changes in backend export service (+ tests).
- Short summary of root cause and fix.
- “Requirements coverage” checklist mapped to this issue.
- Note any manual verification steps still required.
I'll start by reading the export service and the migration to understand the exact mismatch.

1	/// Export service: permission-aware CSV/JSON data exports with masking defaults.
2	///
3	/// By default all exports mask identifying fields (client_name → "****",
4	/// provider_id → masked UUID prefix). The caller must hold `api.export.unmasked`
5	/// permission to receive real identifiers.
6	///
7	/// Every export event is recorded in the export_audit_logs table.
8	
9	use sqlx::MySqlPool;
10	use uuid::Uuid;
11	
12	use crate::application::chaos_service::ChaosService;
13	use crate::application::degradation_service::{DegradationService, TOGGLE_EXPORTS};
14	use crate::domain::error::AppError;
15	use crate::domain::scoring_types::{ExportRequest, ExportResult};
16	use crate::infrastructure::audit::{AuditEntry, AuditService};
17	use crate::infrastructure::permission_cache::PermissionCache;
18	
19	#[derive(Clone)]
20	pub struct ExportService {
21	    pool: MySqlPool,
22	    audit: AuditService,
23	    degradation: DegradationService,
24	}
25	
26	impl ExportService {
27	    pub fn new(pool: MySqlPool, audit: AuditService, degradation: DegradationService) -> Self {
28	        Self { pool, audit, degradation }
29	    }
30	
31	    pub async fn export(
32	        &self,
33	        org_id: &str,
34	        exported_by: &str,
35	        perm_cache: &PermissionCache,
36	        req: &ExportRequest,
37	    ) -> Result<ExportResult, AppError> {
38	        // Check degradation toggle — exports can be centrally disabled
39	        if !self.degradation.get_flag(TOGGLE_EXPORTS).await {
40	            tracing::warn!(actor = exported_by, "Export rejected: exports_enabled=false");
41	            return Err(AppError::ServiceUnavailable(
42	                "Exports are temporarily disabled. Contact your system administrator.".to_string()
43	            ));
44	        }
45	
46	        // Inject chaos latency if a drill is active
47	        ChaosService::maybe_inject_latency().await;
48	
49	        // Validate dates
50	        if chrono::NaiveDate::parse_from_str(&req.from_date, "%Y-%m-%d").is_err() {
51	            return Err(AppError::BadRequest("Invalid from_date format — expected YYYY-MM-DD".to_string()));
52	        }
53	        if chrono::NaiveDate::parse_from_str(&req.to_date, "%Y-%m-%d").is_err() {
54	            return Err(AppError::BadRequest("Invalid to_date format — expected YYYY-MM-DD".to_string()));
55	        }
56	
57	        // Determine masking: default masked unless user has EXPORT_UNMASKED and explicitly requests it
58	        let wants_unmasked = req.unmasked.unwrap_or(false);
59	        let has_unmasked_perm = perm_cache
60	            .has_permission(exported_by, crate::domain::auth_policy::api::EXPORT_UNMASKED)
61	            .await
62	            .unwrap_or(false);
63	        let unmasked = wants_unmasked && has_unmasked_perm;
64	        let masked = !unmasked;
65	
66	        let rows = match req.export_type.as_str() {
67	            "deliveries" => {
68	                self.export_deliveries(org_id, &req.from_date, &req.to_date, req.department_id.as_deref(), req.project_id.as_deref(), masked).await?
69	            }
70	            "evaluations" => {
71	                self.export_evaluations(org_id, &req.from_date, &req.to_date, req.department_id.as_deref(), req.project_id.as_deref(), masked).await?
72	            }
73	            "revenue" => {
74	                self.export_revenue(org_id, &req.from_date, &req.to_date, req.department_id.as_deref(), req.project_id.as_deref(), masked).await?
75	            }
76	            other => {
77	                return Err(AppError::BadRequest(format!(
78	                    "Unknown export_type '{}' — must be 'deliveries', 'evaluations', or 'revenue'",
79	                    other
80	                )));
81	            }
82	        };
83	
84	        let row_count = rows.len();
85	
86	        // Record export audit log
87	        let log_id = Uuid::new_v4().to_string();
88	        let filters_json = serde_json::json!({
89	            "from_date": req.from_date,
90	            "to_date": req.to_date,
91	            "department_id": req.department_id,
92	            "project_id": req.project_id,
93	        })
94	        .to_string();
95	
96	        let permission_used: Option<&str> = if !masked {
97	            Some(crate::domain::auth_policy::api::EXPORT_UNMASKED)
98	        } else {
99	            None
100	        };
101	
102	        sqlx::query(
103	            "INSERT INTO export_audit_logs
104	             (id, org_id, exported_by, export_type, filters_json, row_count, masked, permission_used)
105	             VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)"
106	        )
107	        .bind(&log_id)
108	        .bind(org_id)
109	        .bind(exported_by)
110	        .bind(&req.export_type)
111	        .bind(&filters_json)
112	        .bind(row_count as i32)
113	        .bind(masked)
114	        .bind(permission_used)
115	        .execute(&self.pool)
116	        .await
117	        .map_err(|e| AppError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;
118	
119	        self.audit.log(AuditEntry {
120	            user_id: Some(exported_by.to_string()),
121	            action: "export.performed".to_string(),
122	            resource_type: "export_audit_log".to_string(),
123	            resource_id: Some(log_id.clone()),
124	            org_id: Some(org_id.to_string()),
125	            details: Some(serde_json::json!({
126	                "export_type": req.export_type,
127	                "row_count": row_count,
128	                "masked": masked,
129	            })),
130	            ip_address: None,
131	            trace_id: None,
132	        }).await;
133	
134	        Ok(ExportResult {
135	            rows,
136	            row_count,
137	            masked,
138	            export_log_id: log_id,
139	        })
140	    }
141	
142	    // ------------------------------------------------------------------
143	    // Delivery export
144	    // ------------------------------------------------------------------
145	
146	    async fn export_deliveries(
147	        &self,
148	        org_id: &str,
149	        from_date: &str,
150	        to_date: &str,
151	        department_id: Option<&str>,
152	        project_id: Option<&str>,
153	        masked: bool,
154	    ) -> Result<Vec<serde_json::Value>, AppError> {
155	        let mut extra_clauses = String::new();
156	        if department_id.is_some() {
157	            extra_clauses.push_str(" AND cp.department_id = ?");
158	        }
159	        if project_id.is_some() {
160	            extra_clauses.push_str(" AND cp.project_id = ?");
161	        }
162	
163	        let sql = format!(
164	            "SELECT
165	                de.id,
166	                de.delivery_date,
167	                de.plan_id,
168	                cp.client_name,
169	                de.provider_id,
170	                de.service_item_id,
171	                si.name       AS service_name,
172	                de.units,
173	                de.mileage,
174	                de.status,
175	                de.start_time,
176	                de.end_time
177	             FROM delivery_entries de
178	             JOIN client_plans cp ON cp.id = de.plan_id AND cp.org_id = ?
179	             JOIN service_items si ON si.id = de.service_item_id
180	             WHERE de.delivery_date BETWEEN ? AND ?
181	             {}
182	             ORDER BY de.delivery_date ASC, de.id ASC
183	             LIMIT 10000",
184	            extra_clauses
185	        );
186	
187	        let mut q = sqlx::query_as::<
188	            _,
189	            (
190	                String, String, String, String, String, String, String,
191	                f64, Option<f64>, String, Option<String>, Option<String>,
192	            ),
193	        >(&sql)
194	        .bind(org_id)
195	        .bind(from_date)
196	        .bind(to_date);
197	
198	        if let Some(d) = department_id {
199	            q = q.bind(d);
200	        }
201	        if let Some(p) = project_id {
202	            q = q.bind(p);
203	        }
204	
205	        let rows = q
206	            .fetch_all(&self.pool)
207	            .await
208	            .map_err(|e| AppError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;
209	
210	        Ok(rows
211	            .into_iter()
212	            .map(
213	                |(id, delivery_date, plan_id, client_name, provider_id, service_item_id,
214	                  service_name, units, mileage, status, start_time, end_time)| {
215	                    serde_json::json!({
216	                        "id": id,
217	                        "delivery_date": delivery_date,
218	                        "plan_id": if masked { mask_id(&plan_id) } else { plan_id },
219	                        "client_name": if masked { "****".to_string() } else { client_name },
220	                        "provider_id": if masked { mask_id(&provider_id) } else { provider_id },
221	                        "service_item_id": service_item_id,
222	                        "service_name": service_name,
223	                        "units": units,
224	                        "mileage": mileage,
225	                        "status": status,
226	                        "start_time": start_time,
227	                        "end_time": end_time,
228	                    })
229	                },
230	            )
231	            .collect())
232	    }
233	
234	    // ------------------------------------------------------------------
235	    // Evaluations export
236	    // ------------------------------------------------------------------
237	
238	    async fn export_evaluations(
239	        &self,
240	        org_id: &str,
241	        from_date: &str,
242	        to_date: &str,
243	        department_id: Option<&str>,
244	        project_id: Option<&str>,
245	        masked: bool,
246	    ) -> Result<Vec<serde_json::Value>, AppError> {
247	        // When filtering by department or project we must join through
248	        // delivery_entries → client_plans to reach the scope columns.
249	        let needs_plan_join = department_id.is_some() || project_id.is_some();
250	        let plan_join = if needs_plan_join {
251	            "JOIN delivery_entries de ON de.id = e.delivery_entry_id \
252	             JOIN client_plans cp ON cp.id = de.plan_id"
253	        } else {
254	            ""
255	        };
256	        let mut extra_clauses = String::new();
257	        if department_id.is_some() {
258	            extra_clauses.push_str(" AND cp.department_id = ?");
259	        }
260	        if project_id.is_some() {
261	            extra_clauses.push_str(" AND cp.project_id = ?");
262	        }
263	
264	        let sql = format!(
265	            "SELECT
266	                e.id,
267	                e.delivery_entry_id,
268	                e.template_id,
269	                st.name   AS template_name,
270	                e.evaluator_id,
271	                e.raw_score,
272	                e.weighted_score,
273	                e.final_score,
274	                e.score_delta,
275	                e.status,
276	                e.created_at,
277	                e.updated_at
278	             FROM evaluations e
279	             JOIN scoring_templates st ON st.id = e.template_id
280	             {}
281	             WHERE e.org_id = ?
282	               AND DATE(e.created_at) BETWEEN ? AND ?
283	             {}
284	             ORDER BY e.created_at ASC
285	             LIMIT 10000",
286	            plan_join, extra_clauses
287	        );
288	
289	        let mut q = sqlx::query_as::<
290	            _,
291	            (
292	                String, String, String, String, String, Option<f64>, Option<f64>, Option<f64>,
293	                Option<f64>, String, String, String,
294	            ),
295	        >(&sql)
296	        .bind(org_id)
297	        .bind(from_date)
298	        .bind(to_date);
299	
300	        if let Some(d) = department_id {
301	            q = q.bind(d);
302	        }
303	        if let Some(p) = project_id {
304	            q = q.bind(p);
305	        }
306	
307	        let rows = q
308	            .fetch_all(&self.pool)
309	            .await
310	            .map_err(|e| AppError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;
311	
312	        Ok(rows
313	            .into_iter()
314	            .map(
315	                |(id, delivery_entry_id, template_id, template_name, evaluator_id,
316	                  raw_score, weighted_score, final_score, score_delta, status, created_at, updated_at)| {
317	                    serde_json::json!({
318	                        "id": id,
319	                        "delivery_entry_id": delivery_entry_id,
320	                        "template_id": template_id,
321	                        "template_name": template_name,
322	                        "evaluator_id": if masked { mask_id(&evaluator_id) } else { evaluator_id },
323	                        "raw_score": raw_score,
324	                        "weighted_score": weighted_score,
325	                        "final_score": final_score,
326	                        "score_delta": score_delta,
327	                        "status": status,
328	                        "created_at": created_at,
329	                        "updated_at": updated_at,
330	                    })
331	                },
332	            )
333	            .collect())
334	    }
335	
336	    // ------------------------------------------------------------------
337	    // Revenue export
338	    // ------------------------------------------------------------------
339	
340	    async fn export_revenue(
341	        &self,
342	        org_id: &str,
343	        from_date: &str,
344	        to_date: &str,
345	        department_id: Option<&str>,
346	        project_id: Option<&str>,
347	        masked: bool,
348	    ) -> Result<Vec<serde_json::Value>, AppError> {
349	        let mut extra_clauses = String::new();
350	        if department_id.is_some() {
351	            extra_clauses.push_str(" AND cp.department_id = ?");
352	        }
353	        if project_id.is_some() {
354	            extra_clauses.push_str(" AND cp.project_id = ?");
355	        }
356	
357	        let sql = format!(
358	            "SELECT
359	                i.id,
360	                i.invoice_number,
361	                i.plan_id,
362	                cp.client_name,
363	                i.subtotal,
364	                i.total_adjustments,
365	                i.total_amount,
366	                i.status,
367	                i.billing_period_start,
368	                i.billing_period_end
369	             FROM invoices i
370	             JOIN client_plans cp ON cp.id = i.plan_id AND cp.org_id = ?
371	             WHERE DATE(i.created_at) BETWEEN ? AND ?
372	             {}
373	             ORDER BY i.created_at ASC
374	             LIMIT 10000",
375	            extra_clauses
376	        );
377	
378	        let mut q = sqlx::query_as::<
379	            _,
380	            (String, String, String, String, f64, f64, f64, String, String, String),
381	        >(&sql)
382	        .bind(org_id)
383	        .bind(from_date)
384	        .bind(to_date);
385	
386	        if let Some(d) = department_id {
387	            q = q.bind(d);
388	        }
389	        if let Some(p) = project_id {
390	            q = q.bind(p);
391	        }
392	
393	        let rows = q
394	            .fetch_all(&self.pool)
395	            .await
396	            .map_err(|e| AppError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;
397	
398	        Ok(rows
399	            .into_iter()
400	            .map(
401	                |(id, invoice_number, plan_id, client_name, subtotal,
402	                  total_adjustments, total_amount, status, billing_period_start, billing_period_end)| {
403	                    serde_json::json!({
404	                        "id": id,
405	                        "invoice_number": invoice_number,
406	                        "plan_id": if masked { mask_id(&plan_id) } else { plan_id },
407	                        "client_name": if masked { "****".to_string() } else { client_name },
408	                        "subtotal": subtotal,
409	                        "total_adjustments": total_adjustments,
410	                        "total_amount": total_amount,
411	                        "status": status,
412	                        "billing_period_start": billing_period_start,
413	                        "billing_period_end": billing_period_end,
414	                    })
415	                },
416	            )
417	            .collect())
418	    }
419	}
420	
421	/// Returns the first 8 chars of a UUID-style id, rest replaced with "****".
422	/// e.g. "550e8400-e29b-..." → "550e8400-****"
423	fn mask_id(id: &str) -> String {
424	    if id.len() <= 8 {
425	        return "****".to_string();
426	    }
427	    format!("{}-****", &id[..8])
428	}

1	-- CareOps Phase 3: Service Catalog, Packages, Client Plans, Delivery
2	-- Migration: 20240103000000_catalog_packages_plans_delivery
3	--
4	-- Replaces/extends Phase 1 stub tables with production schema for:
5	--   - Typed service catalog items
6	--   - Package definitions with per-visit/hourly/tiered billing rules
7	--   - Client plan lifecycle with package assignments
8	--   - Delivery entry capture with validation constraints
9	--   - Eligibility notes with encryption support
10	
11	-- ============================================================
12	-- Drop Phase 1 stub tables that are being replaced
13	-- (delivery_entries has FK to client_plans, so drop in dependency order)
14	-- ============================================================
15	DROP TABLE IF EXISTS quality_scores;
16	DROP TABLE IF EXISTS payments;
17	DROP TABLE IF EXISTS invoices;
18	DROP TABLE IF EXISTS delivery_entries;
19	DROP TABLE IF EXISTS package_services;
20	DROP TABLE IF EXISTS client_plans;
21	DROP TABLE IF EXISTS packages;
22	DROP TABLE IF EXISTS service_catalog;
23	
24	-- ============================================================
25	-- Service Catalog Items
26	-- ============================================================
27	CREATE TABLE service_catalog_items (
28	    id CHAR(36) PRIMARY KEY,
29	    org_id CHAR(36) NOT NULL,
30	    code VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
31	    name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
32	    description TEXT,
33	    category ENUM('nursing', 'rehab', 'meals', 'companionship', 'transportation', 'other') NOT NULL,
34	    unit_type ENUM('visit', 'hour', 'mile', 'meal', 'session') NOT NULL,
35	    default_rate DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
36	    is_active TINYINT(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
37	    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
38	    updated_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
39	    FOREIGN KEY (org_id) REFERENCES organizations(id),
40	    UNIQUE KEY uq_catalog_org_code (org_id, code)
41	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
42	
43	-- ============================================================
44	-- Package Definitions
45	-- ============================================================
46	CREATE TABLE package_definitions (
47	    id CHAR(36) PRIMARY KEY,
48	    org_id CHAR(36) NOT NULL,
49	    code VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
50	    name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
51	    description TEXT,
52	    is_active TINYINT(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
53	    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
54	    updated_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
55	    FOREIGN KEY (org_id) REFERENCES organizations(id),

[ "$FAILED" -eq 0 ] || exit 1", "new_string": "# =============================================================
# Delivery export regression (service_catalog_items join fix)
#
# The export_type=deliveries query JOINs the service catalog table
# to resolve service_name.  A prior bug used a non-existent table
# alias ("service_items") causing a runtime SQL error.  These tests
# assert the query succeeds and the response contains the expected
# fields, preventing regression.
# =============================================================

echo ""
echo "[17] Delivery export returns 200 and expected fields"

DEL_EXPORT=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/reports/export" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{
        "export_type": "deliveries",
        "from_date": "2024-01-01",
        "to_date": "2024-12-31"
    }' 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")

DEL_STATUS=$(echo "$DEL_EXPORT" | python3 -c "
import sys, json
try:
    d = json.load(sys.stdin)
    # Must have these top-level keys
    assert 'rows' in d, 'missing rows'
    assert 'row_count' in d, 'missing row_count'
    assert 'masked' in d, 'missing masked'
    assert 'export_log_id' in d, 'missing export_log_id'
    print('OK')
except Exception as e:
    print(f'FAIL:{e}')
" 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL:parse_error")
check "Delivery export response shape" "OK" "$DEL_STATUS"

# If rows exist, verify each row has the expected delivery-export fields
DEL_FIELDS=$(echo "$DEL_EXPORT" | python3 -c "
import sys, json
try:
    d = json.load(sys.stdin)
    rows = d.get('rows', [])
    if not rows:
        print('OK_EMPTY')
        sys.exit(0)
    expected = {'id','delivery_date','plan_id','client_name','provider_id',
                'service_item_id','service_name','units','mileage','status'}
    for i, r in enumerate(rows):
        missing = expected - set(r.keys())
        if missing:
            print(f'FAIL:row[{i}] missing {missing}')
            sys.exit(0)
    print('OK')
except Exception as e:
    print(f'FAIL:{e}')
" 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL:parse_error")

if [ "$DEL_FIELDS" = "OK" ] || [ "$DEL_FIELDS" = "OK_EMPTY" ]; then
    check "Delivery export row fields present" "1" "1"
else
    check "Delivery export row fields present" "OK" "$DEL_FIELDS"
fi

echo "[18] Delivery export row_count matches rows array length"

DEL_COUNT_CHECK=$(echo "$DEL_EXPORT" | python3 -c "
import sys, json
try:
    d = json.load(sys.stdin)
    actual_len = len(d.get('rows', []))
    declared = d.get('row_count', -1)
    if actual_len == declared:
        print('OK')
    else:
        print(f'FAIL:row_count={declared},actual={actual_len}')
except Exception as e:
    print(f'FAIL:{e}')
" 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL:parse_error")

Clean. Here's the summary:

---

## Root Cause

`export_service.rs:179` had `JOIN service_items si` — but no table named `service_items` exists. The actual table created by migration `20240103` is `service_catalog_items`. This caused any `export_type=deliveries` request to fail at runtime with a MySQL "table doesn't exist" error, returned to the client as a 500 Internal Server Error.

Every other service in the codebase (`catalog_service.rs`, `billing_service.rs`, `package_service.rs`) correctly references `service_catalog_items`. This was an isolated typo in the export service.

## Fix

Single-character-class change: `service_items` → `service_catalog_items` at line 179. All column references (`si.id`, `si.name`) are valid on the corrected table.

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `backend/src/application/export_service.rs:179` | `JOIN service_items si` → `JOIN service_catalog_items si` |
| `API_tests/test_reports.sh` | Added tests [17-19]: delivery export response shape validation, field presence per row, row_count consistency, and filtered export still returns 200 |

## Requirements Coverage Checklist

| Requirement | Status |
|---|---|
| SQL join uses correct table name (`service_catalog_items`) | Fixed |
| All referenced columns (`si.id`, `si.name`) exist with same semantics | Verified — both exist in `service_catalog_items` per migration |
| No breaking API changes (routes, JSON fields, status codes) | No changes — same SELECT columns, same response shape |
| Export masking logic unchanged | Not touched — `mask_id()` and client_name masking intact |
| Department/project filter behavior unchanged | Not touched — `extra_clauses` logic identical |
| Test: deliveries export returns 200 with correct response shape | Test [17] |
| Test: delivery rows contain expected fields (`service_name`, etc.) | Test [17] field check |
| Test: `row_count` matches `rows` array length | Test [18] |
| Test: filtered delivery export returns 200 | Test [19] |
| No docs reference old table name | Verified — `grep` found no matches in `docs/` |

## Manual Verification Steps

- Run the full test suite against a live stack: `bash API_tests/test_reports.sh`
- Confirm test [8] (which also calls `export_type=deliveries`) now succeeds with actual rows if delivery entries exist in the date range, rather than returning a 500 error

Non-breaking constraints:

Implementation guidance:

2) Backend:

Acceptance criteria:
