content.text:
1	/// Reporting service: order volume, revenue, utilization, KPI analytics.
2	/// All results are derived from real stored data. No mocks.
3	///
4	/// Reports accept ISO date strings (YYYY-MM-DD) for from_date and to_date,
5	/// and optional department_id / project_id filters.
6	
7	use chrono::NaiveDate;
8	use sqlx::MySqlPool;
9	
10	use crate::application::chaos_service::ChaosService;
11	use crate::application::degradation_service::{DegradationService, TOGGLE_ANALYTICS};
12	use crate::domain::error::AppError;
13	use crate::domain::scoring_types::{
14	    KpiSummary, OrderVolumeRow, ReportFilters, RevenueReportRow, UtilizationRow,
15	};
16	
17	#[derive(Clone)]
18	pub struct ReportService {
19	    pool: MySqlPool,
20	    degradation: DegradationService,
21	}
22	
23	impl ReportService {
24	    pub fn new(pool: MySqlPool, degradation: DegradationService) -> Self {
25	        Self { pool, degradation }
26	    }
27	
28	    async fn check_analytics_enabled(&self) -> Result<(), AppError> {
29	        if !self.degradation.get_flag(TOGGLE_ANALYTICS).await {
30	            tracing::warn!("Report rejected: analytics_enabled=false");
31	            return Err(AppError::ServiceUnavailable(
32	                "Heavy analytics are temporarily disabled. Contact your system administrator.".to_string()
33	            ));
34	        }
35	        ChaosService::maybe_inject_latency().await;
36	        Ok(())
37	    }
38	
39	    fn validate_date_filters(filters: &ReportFilters) -> Result<(), AppError> {
40	        NaiveDate::parse_from_str(&filters.from_date, "%Y-%m-%d")
41	            .map_err(|_| AppError::BadRequest("Invalid from_date format — expected YYYY-MM-DD".to_string()))?;
42	        NaiveDate::parse_from_str(&filters.to_date, "%Y-%m-%d")
43	            .map_err(|_| AppError::BadRequest("Invalid to_date format — expected YYYY-MM-DD".to_string()))?;
44	        Ok(())
45	    }
46	
47	    // ------------------------------------------------------------------
48	    // Order volume: deliveries grouped by week (YYYY-WNN)
49	    // ------------------------------------------------------------------
50	
51	    pub async fn order_volume(
52	        &self,
53	        org_id: &str,
54	        filters: &ReportFilters,
55	    ) -> Result<Vec<OrderVolumeRow>, AppError> {
56	        Self::validate_date_filters(filters)?;
57	        self.check_analytics_enabled().await?;
58	        // Build optional WHERE fragments for department and project
59	        let dept_join = if filters.department_id.is_some() {
60	            "JOIN client_plans cp2 ON cp2.id = de.plan_id AND cp2.department_id = ?"
61	        } else {
62	            ""
63	        };
64	        let project_join = if filters.project_id.is_some() {
65	            "JOIN client_plans cp3 ON cp3.id = de.plan_id AND cp3.project_id = ?"
66	        } else {
67	            ""
68	        };
69	
70	        let sql = format!(
71	            "SELECT
72	                DATE_FORMAT(de.delivery_date, '%Y-W%v') AS period,
73	                COUNT(de.id)                              AS delivery_count,
74	                COUNT(DISTINCT de.plan_id)                AS unique_plans,
75	                COUNT(DISTINCT de.provider_id)            AS unique_providers
76	             FROM delivery_entries de
77	             JOIN client_plans cp ON cp.id = de.plan_id AND cp.org_id = ?
78	             {} {}
79	             WHERE de.delivery_date BETWEEN ? AND ?
80	             GROUP BY period
81	             ORDER BY period ASC
82	             LIMIT ? OFFSET ?",
83	            dept_join, project_join
84	        );
85	
86	        let mut q = sqlx::query_as::<_, (String, i64, i64, i64)>(&sql).bind(org_id);
87	        if let Some(d) = &filters.department_id { q = q.bind(d); }
88	        if let Some(p) = &filters.project_id   { q = q.bind(p); }
89	        let q = q
90	            .bind(&filters.from_date)
91	            .bind(&filters.to_date)
92	            .bind(filters.limit.unwrap_or(200))
93	            .bind(filters.offset.unwrap_or(0));
94	
95	        let rows = q
96	            .fetch_all(&self.pool)
97	            .await
98	            .map_err(|e| AppError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;
99	
100	        Ok(rows
101	            .into_iter()
102	            .map(|(period, delivery_count, unique_plans, unique_providers)| OrderVolumeRow {
103	                period,
104	                delivery_count,
105	                unique_plans,
106	                unique_providers,
107	            })
108	            .collect())
109	    }
110	
111	    // ------------------------------------------------------------------
112	    // Revenue report: invoice/payment/refund aggregates by week
113	    // ------------------------------------------------------------------
114	
115	    pub async fn revenue_report(
116	        &self,
117	        org_id: &str,
118	        filters: &ReportFilters,
119	    ) -> Result<Vec<RevenueReportRow>, AppError> {
120	        Self::validate_date_filters(filters)?;
121	        self.check_analytics_enabled().await?;
122	        let dept_join = if filters.department_id.is_some() {
123	            "JOIN client_plans cp2 ON cp2.id = i.plan_id AND cp2.department_id = ?"
124	        } else {
125	            ""
126	        };
127	        let project_join = if filters.project_id.is_some() {
128	            "JOIN client_plans cp3 ON cp3.id = i.plan_id AND cp3.project_id = ?"
129	        } else {
130	            ""
131	        };
132	
133	        let sql = format!(
134	            "SELECT
135	                DATE_FORMAT(i.created_at, '%Y-W%v')  AS period,
136	                COALESCE(SUM(i.subtotal), 0)          AS gross_charges,
137	                COALESCE(SUM(i.total_amount), 0)      AS net_charges,
138	                COALESCE(SUM(i.total_amount), 0)      AS total_invoiced,
139	                COALESCE(SUM(
140	                    (SELECT COALESCE(SUM(rp.amount), 0)
141	                     FROM recorded_payments rp WHERE rp.invoice_id = i.id)
142	                ), 0)                                 AS total_paid,
143	                COALESCE(SUM(
144	                    (SELECT COALESCE(SUM(rr.amount), 0)
145	                     FROM recorded_refunds rr WHERE rr.invoice_id = i.id)
146	                ), 0)                                 AS total_refunded
147	             FROM invoices i
148	             JOIN client_plans cp ON cp.id = i.plan_id AND cp.org_id = ?
149	             {} {}
150	             WHERE DATE(i.created_at) BETWEEN ? AND ?
151	             GROUP BY period
152	             ORDER BY period ASC
153	             LIMIT ? OFFSET ?",
154	            dept_join, project_join
155	        );
156	
157	        let mut q = sqlx::query_as::<_, (String, f64, f64, f64, f64, f64)>(&sql).bind(org_id);
158	        if let Some(d) = &filters.department_id { q = q.bind(d); }
159	        if let Some(p) = &filters.project_id   { q = q.bind(p); }
160	        let q = q
161	            .bind(&filters.from_date)
162	            .bind(&filters.to_date)
163	            .bind(filters.limit.unwrap_or(200))
164	            .bind(filters.offset.unwrap_or(0));
165	
166	        let rows = q
167	            .fetch_all(&self.pool)
168	            .await
169	            .map_err(|e| AppError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;
170	
171	        Ok(rows
172	            .into_iter()
173	            .map(|(period, gross_charges, net_charges, total_invoiced, total_paid, total_refunded)| {
174	                let refund_rate_pct = if total_paid > 0.0 {
175	                    (total_refunded / total_paid) * 100.0
176	                } else {
177	                    0.0
178	                };
179	                RevenueReportRow {
180	                    period,
181	                    gross_charges,
182	                    net_charges,
183	                    total_invoiced,
184	                    total_paid,
185	                    total_refunded,
186	                    refund_rate_pct: round_2dp(refund_rate_pct),
187	                }
188	            })
189	            .collect())
190	    }
191	
192	    // ------------------------------------------------------------------
193	    // Provider utilization: visits/units/mileage per provider per week
194	    // ------------------------------------------------------------------
195	
196	    pub async fn utilization_report(
197	        &self,
198	        org_id: &str,
199	        filters: &ReportFilters,
200	    ) -> Result<Vec<UtilizationRow>, AppError> {
201	        Self::validate_date_filters(filters)?;
202	        self.check_analytics_enabled().await?;
203	        let dept_join = if filters.department_id.is_some() {
204	            "JOIN client_plans cp2 ON cp2.id = de.plan_id AND cp2.department_id = ?"
205	        } else {
206	            ""
207	        };
208	        let project_join = if filters.project_id.is_some() {
209	            "JOIN client_plans cp3 ON cp3.id = de.plan_id AND cp3.project_id = ?"
210	        } else {
211	            ""
212	        };
213	
214	        let sql = format!(
215	            "SELECT
216	                de.provider_id,
217	                DATE_FORMAT(de.delivery_date, '%Y-W%v') AS period,
218	                COUNT(de.id)                             AS total_visits,
219	                COALESCE(SUM(de.units), 0)               AS total_units,
220	                COALESCE(SUM(de.mileage), 0)             AS total_mileage
221	             FROM delivery_entries de
222	             JOIN client_plans cp ON cp.id = de.plan_id AND cp.org_id = ?
223	             {} {}
224	             WHERE de.delivery_date BETWEEN ? AND ?
225	               AND de.status = 'verified'
226	             GROUP BY de.provider_id, period
227	             ORDER BY period ASC, de.provider_id ASC
228	             LIMIT ? OFFSET ?",
229	            dept_join, project_join
230	        );
231	
232	        let mut q = sqlx::query_as::<_, (String, String, i64, f64, f64)>(&sql).bind(org_id);
233	        if let Some(d) = &filters.department_id { q = q.bind(d); }
234	        if let Some(p) = &filters.project_id   { q = q.bind(p); }
235	        let q = q
236	            .bind(&filters.from_date)
237	            .bind(&filters.to_date)
238	            .bind(filters.limit.unwrap_or(500))
239	            .bind(filters.offset.unwrap_or(0));
240	
241	        let rows = q
242	            .fetch_all(&self.pool)
243	            .await
244	            .map_err(|e| AppError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;
245	
246	        Ok(rows
247	            .into_iter()
248	            .map(|(provider_id, period, total_visits, total_units, total_mileage)| UtilizationRow {
249	                provider_id,
250	                period,
251	                total_visits,
252	                total_units,
253	                total_mileage,
254	            })
255	            .collect())
256	    }
257	
258	    // ------------------------------------------------------------------
259	    // KPI summary
260	    // Attendance rate: verified / (submitted + verified)
261	    // Repurchase rate: plans with 2+ distinct billing periods / total plans
262	    // Staff utilization: avg deliveries per active provider vs capacity proxy (20/week)
263	    // ------------------------------------------------------------------
264	
265	    pub async fn kpi_summary(
266	        &self,
267	        org_id: &str,
268	        filters: &ReportFilters,
269	    ) -> Result<KpiSummary, AppError> {
270	        Self::validate_date_filters(filters)?;
271	        self.check_analytics_enabled().await?;
272	        // 1. Attendance rate
273	        let (submitted_count, verified_count): (i64, i64) = {
274	            let row: (i64, i64) = sqlx::query_as(
275	                "SELECT
276	                    SUM(CASE WHEN de.status = 'submitted' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END),
277	                    SUM(CASE WHEN de.status = 'verified'  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
278	                 FROM delivery_entries de
279	                 JOIN client_plans cp ON cp.id = de.plan_id AND cp.org_id = ?
280	                 WHERE de.delivery_date BETWEEN ? AND ?"
281	            )
282	            .bind(org_id)
283	            .bind(&filters.from_date)
284	            .bind(&filters.to_date)
285	            .fetch_one(&self.pool)
286	            .await
287	            .map_err(|e| AppError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;
288	            row
289	        };
290	
291	        let attendance_rate_pct = {
292	            let total = submitted_count + verified_count;
293	            if total > 0 {
294	                (verified_count as f64 / total as f64) * 100.0
295	            } else {
296	                0.0
297	            }
298	        };
299	
300	        // 2. Repurchase rate: plans with 2+ invoice billing periods / total plans
301	        let (plans_with_repeat, total_plans): (i64, i64) = {
302	            let row: (i64, i64) = sqlx::query_as(
303	                "SELECT
304	                    SUM(CASE WHEN invoice_periods >= 2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END),
305	                    COUNT(*)
306	                 FROM (
307	                    SELECT cp.id, COUNT(DISTINCT DATE_FORMAT(i.created_at, '%Y-%m')) AS invoice_periods
308	                    FROM client_plans cp
309	                    LEFT JOIN invoices i ON i.plan_id = cp.id
310	                        AND DATE(i.created_at) BETWEEN ? AND ?
311	                    WHERE cp.org_id = ?
312	                    GROUP BY cp.id
313	                 ) plan_periods"
314	            )
315	            .bind(&filters.from_date)
316	            .bind(&filters.to_date)
317	            .bind(org_id)
318	            .fetch_one(&self.pool)
319	            .await
320	            .map_err(|e| AppError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;
321	            row
322	        };
323	
324	        let repurchase_rate_pct = if total_plans > 0 {
325	            (plans_with_repeat as f64 / total_plans as f64) * 100.0
326	        } else {
327	            0.0
328	        };
329	
330	        // 3. Staff utilization: avg deliveries per active provider / 20 (capacity proxy per week)
331	        let (total_deliveries, provider_count): (i64, i64) = {
332	            let row: (i64, i64) = sqlx::query_as(
333	                "SELECT COUNT(de.id), COUNT(DISTINCT de.provider_id)
334	                 FROM delivery_entries de
335	                 JOIN client_plans cp ON cp.id = de.plan_id AND cp.org_id = ?
336	                 WHERE de.delivery_date BETWEEN ? AND ?"
337	            )
338	            .bind(org_id)
339	            .bind(&filters.from_date)
340	            .bind(&filters.to_date)
341	            .fetch_one(&self.pool)
342	            .await
343	            .map_err(|e| AppError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;
344	            row
345	        };
346	
347	        // Approximate weeks in range
348	        let weeks: f64 = {
349	            let from = chrono::NaiveDate::parse_from_str(&filters.from_date, "%Y-%m-%d")
350	                .unwrap_or_default();
351	            let to = chrono::NaiveDate::parse_from_str(&filters.to_date, "%Y-%m-%d")
352	                .unwrap_or_default();
353	            let days = (to - from).num_days().max(1) as f64;
354	            (days / 7.0).ceil()
355	        };
356	
357	        let staff_utilization_pct = if provider_count > 0 && weeks > 0.0 {
358	            let avg_per_provider_per_week = total_deliveries as f64 / provider_count as f64 / weeks;
359	            // Capacity proxy: 20 deliveries per week = 100%
360	            (avg_per_provider_per_week / 20.0 * 100.0).min(100.0)
361	        } else {
362	            0.0
363	        };
364	
365	        // 4. Average quality score
366	        let avg_score: Option<f64> = {
367	            let row: (Option<f64>,) = sqlx::query_as(
368	                "SELECT AVG(e.final_score)
369	                 FROM evaluations e
370	                 WHERE e.org_id = ?
371	                   AND e.status = 'finalized'
372	                   AND DATE(e.updated_at) BETWEEN ? AND ?"
373	            )
374	            .bind(org_id)
375	            .bind(&filters.from_date)
376	            .bind(&filters.to_date)
377	            .fetch_one(&self.pool)
378	            .await
379	            .map_err(|e| AppError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;
380	            row.0
381	        };
382	
383	        // 5. Second review rate
384	        let (total_evals, second_review_evals): (i64, i64) = {
385	            let row: (i64, i64) = sqlx::query_as(
386	                "SELECT COUNT(*), SUM(CASE WHEN requires_second_review = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
387	                 FROM evaluations
388	                 WHERE org_id = ?
389	                   AND DATE(created_at) BETWEEN ? AND ?"
390	            )
391	            .bind(org_id)
392	            .bind(&filters.from_date)
393	            .bind(&filters.to_date)
394	            .fetch_one(&self.pool)
395	            .await
396	            .map_err(|e| AppError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;
397	            row
398	        };
399	
400	        let second_review_rate_pct = if total_evals > 0 {
401	            (second_review_evals as f64 / total_evals as f64) * 100.0
402	        } else {
403	            0.0
404	        };
405	
406	        Ok(KpiSummary {
407	            period_start: filters.from_date.clone(),
408	            period_end: filters.to_date.clone(),
409	            // NOTE: all _pct values are already on the 0–100 percentage scale
410	            // (ratio × 100 was applied above).  round_2dp rounds to 2 decimal
411	            // places — it does NOT convert to percentage again.
412	            attendance_rate_pct: round_2dp(attendance_rate_pct),
413	            repurchase_rate_pct: round_2dp(repurchase_rate_pct),
414	            staff_utilization_pct: round_2dp(staff_utilization_pct),
415	            avg_score: avg_score.map(round_2dp),
416	            second_review_rate_pct: round_2dp(second_review_rate_pct),
417	        })
418	    }
419	}
420	
421	/// Round an f64 to two decimal places.  Used for KPI output formatting.
422	/// This is purely a rounding operation — it does NOT convert fractions to
423	/// percentages.  Values are expected to already be on their final scale.
424	fn round_2dp(v: f64) -> f64 {
425	    (v * 100.0).round() / 100.0
426	}
427

Perfect! Now let me compile all the information you requested. Here's a comprehensive summary:

## File Contents Summary

### 1. Reports API Route Handler
**Path:** `/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/CareOps/TASK-53/repo/backend/src/api/reports_exports/mod.rs`

The Reports & Exports API provides 5 endpoints:
- `GET /order-volume` - delivery counts by week
- `GET /revenue` - invoiced/paid/refunded aggregates by week  
- `GET /utilization` - provider visits/units/mileage by week
- `GET /kpi` - KPI summary (attendance, repurchase, utilization, avg score)
- `POST /export` - permission-aware data export with masking

All endpoints require `api.reports.read` permission. The export endpoint requires `action.EXPORT_DATA` permission and supports optional department_id and project_id filters. Data scope validation is enforced via `require_data_scope` guards.

### 2. Frontend Reports Page
**Path:** `/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/CareOps/TASK-53/repo/frontend/src/pages/reports/mod.rs`

A Dioxus-based React-like frontend with 5 tabs:
- **KPI Tab**: Displays 5 metric cards (attendance_rate_pct, repurchase_rate_pct, staff_utilization_pct, avg_score, second_review_rate_pct)
- **Order Volume Tab**: Table with Week, Deliveries, Unique Plans, Unique Providers
- **Revenue Tab**: Table with Week, Gross Charges, Net Charges, Invoiced, Paid, Refunded, Refund Rate
- **Utilization Tab**: Table with Provider, Week, Visits, Units, Mileage
- **Export Tab**: Form for exporting deliveries/evaluations/revenue with masking controls and preview

Permission checks: `api.reports.read`, `action.reports.export`, `api.export.unmasked`

### 3. Export Service
**Path:** `/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/CareOps/TASK-53/repo/backend/src/application/export_service.rs`

The `export_deliveries` method (lines 146-232) currently accepts department_id and project_id parameters. It conditionally builds SQL WHERE clauses if either filter is present:
```sql
if department_id.is_some():
  extra_clauses.push_str(" AND cp.department_id = ?");
if project_id.is_some():
  extra_clauses.push_str(" AND cp.project_id = ?");
```

**Critical finding:** The `export_deliveries` method DOES NOT have route filtering applied. It only filters by org_id, date range, and optionally department/project. The same is true for `export_evaluations` and `export_revenue` methods - they lack route filtering.

### 4. Migrations Directory Listing
Migrations in `/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/CareOps/TASK-53/repo/backend/migrations/`:
- 20240101000000_initial_schema.sql
- 20240102000000_security_rbac_audit.sql
- 20240103000000_catalog_packages_plans_delivery.sql
- 20240104000000_billing_payments_refunds.sql
- 20240105000000_scoring_reporting.sql
- 20240106000000_ops_config.sql
- 20240107000000_payment_idempotency_unique.sql
- 20240108000000_restore_idempotency_window.sql

### 5. API Test Suite
**Path:** `/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/CareOps/TASK-53/repo/API_tests/test_reports.sh`

Comprehensive bash test suite with 19 test sections covering:
- Authentication setup (admin, auditor, coach tokens)
- Unauthenticated access (401 checks)
- Permission enforcement (coach cannot access)
- KPI summary with numeric range assertions (0-100%)
- Order volume, revenue, utilization reports
- Export masking defaults (client_name → "****")
- Export validation (invalid types, date formats)
- Unmasked export gating (requires permission)
- Cross-project scope enforcement (403 for out-of-scope projects)
- Delivery export regression tests (service_catalog_items join)
- Backward compatibility (omitting project_id still works)

### 6. Documentation
**Path:** `/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/CareOps/TASK-53/repo/docs/scoring_and_reporting.md`

Comprehensive documentation covering:
- Scoring workflow with state machine (draft → finalized or second_review_required)
- Scoring formulas (auto_score, manual_score, weighted aggregation, rounding)
- Second review rule (triggered when |new_score - prior_score| > 10)
- Database tables (scoring_templates, evaluation_questions, evaluations, evaluation_answers, score_reviews, export_audit_logs)
- API reference for templates, evaluations, second reviews, and reports
- KPI definitions (attendance rate, repurchase rate, staff utilization, avg quality score, second review rate)
- Export masking behavior and audit trail
- Role access matrix
- Unit test specifications

### 7. Client Plans Table Definition
**Path:** `/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/CareOps/TASK-53/repo/backend/migrations/20240103000000_catalog_packages_plans_delivery.sql` (lines 89-107)

```sql
CREATE TABLE client_plans (
    id CHAR(36) PRIMARY KEY,
    org_id CHAR(36) NOT NULL,
    department_id CHAR(36),
    project_id CHAR(36),
    client_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    client_identifier_enc TEXT,
    status ENUM('draft', 'active', 'paused', 'completed', 'cancelled'),
    start_date DATE NOT NULL,
    end_date DATE,
    notes_enc TEXT,
    created_by CHAR(36),
    created_at TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (org_id) REFERENCES organizations(id),
    FOREIGN KEY (department_id) REFERENCES departments(id),
    FOREIGN KEY (project_id) REFERENCES projects(id),
    FOREIGN KEY (created_by) REFERENCES users(id)
);
```

The table has `department_id` and `project_id` columns for scope filtering, both nullable.

1	/// Reports & Exports API
2	///
3	/// Endpoints:
4	///   GET /order-volume   - delivery counts by week with optional department/project filter
5	///   GET /revenue        - invoiced/paid/refunded aggregates by week
6	///   GET /utilization    - provider visits/units/mileage by week
7	///   GET /kpi            - attendance rate, repurchase rate, staff utilization, avg score
8	///   POST /export        - permission-aware data export (masked by default)
9	
10	use rocket::serde::json::Json;
11	use rocket::{Route, State};
12	
13	use crate::api::guards::{self, AuthenticatedUser};
14	use crate::application::export_service::ExportService;
15	use crate::application::report_service::ReportService;
16	use crate::domain::auth_policy::{action, api};
17	use crate::domain::error::AppError;
18	use crate::domain::scoring_types::*;
19	use crate::infrastructure::permission_cache::PermissionCache;
20	
21	// ============================================================
22	// Order volume
23	// ============================================================
24	
25	#[rocket::get("/order-volume?<from_date>&<to_date>&<department_id>&<project_id>&<limit>&<offset>")]
26	pub async fn order_volume(
27	    from_date: String,
28	    to_date: String,
29	    department_id: Option<String>,
30	    project_id: Option<String>,
31	    limit: Option<i64>,
32	    offset: Option<i64>,
33	    user: AuthenticatedUser,
34	    perm_cache: &State<PermissionCache>,
35	    report: &State<ReportService>,
36	) -> Result<Json<Vec<OrderVolumeRow>>, AppError> {
37	    guards::require_permission(perm_cache, &user.user_id, api::REPORTS_READ).await?;
38	    guards::require_data_scope(perm_cache, &user.user_id, &user.org_id, department_id.as_deref(), project_id.as_deref(), "read").await?;
39	    let filters = ReportFilters { from_date, to_date, department_id, project_id, limit, offset };
40	    let rows = report.order_volume(&user.org_id, &filters).await?;
41	    Ok(Json(rows))
42	}
43	
44	// ============================================================
45	// Revenue report
46	// ============================================================
47	
48	#[rocket::get("/revenue?<from_date>&<to_date>&<department_id>&<project_id>&<limit>&<offset>")]
49	pub async fn revenue_report(
50	    from_date: String,
51	    to_date: String,
52	    department_id: Option<String>,
53	    project_id: Option<String>,
54	    limit: Option<i64>,
55	    offset: Option<i64>,
56	    user: AuthenticatedUser,
57	    perm_cache: &State<PermissionCache>,
58	    report: &State<ReportService>,
59	) -> Result<Json<Vec<RevenueReportRow>>, AppError> {
60	    guards::require_permission(perm_cache, &user.user_id, api::REPORTS_READ).await?;
61	    guards::require_data_scope(perm_cache, &user.user_id, &user.org_id, department_id.as_deref(), project_id.as_deref(), "read").await?;
62	    let filters = ReportFilters { from_date, to_date, department_id, project_id, limit, offset };
63	    let rows = report.revenue_report(&user.org_id, &filters).await?;
64	    Ok(Json(rows))
65	}
66	
67	// ============================================================
68	// Utilization report
69	// ============================================================
70	
71	#[rocket::get("/utilization?<from_date>&<to_date>&<department_id>&<project_id>&<limit>&<offset>")]
72	pub async fn utilization_report(
73	    from_date: String,
74	    to_date: String,
75	    department_id: Option<String>,
76	    project_id: Option<String>,
77	    limit: Option<i64>,
78	    offset: Option<i64>,
79	    user: AuthenticatedUser,
80	    perm_cache: &State<PermissionCache>,
81	    report: &State<ReportService>,
82	) -> Result<Json<Vec<UtilizationRow>>, AppError> {
83	    guards::require_permission(perm_cache, &user.user_id, api::REPORTS_READ).await?;
84	    guards::require_data_scope(perm_cache, &user.user_id, &user.org_id, department_id.as_deref(), project_id.as_deref(), "read").await?;
85	    let filters = ReportFilters { from_date, to_date, department_id, project_id, limit, offset };
86	    let rows = report.utilization_report(&user.org_id, &filters).await?;
87	    Ok(Json(rows))
88	}
89	
90	// ============================================================
91	// KPI summary
92	// ============================================================
93	
94	#[rocket::get("/kpi?<from_date>&<to_date>&<department_id>&<project_id>")]
95	pub async fn kpi_summary(
96	    from_date: String,
97	    to_date: String,
98	    department_id: Option<String>,
99	    project_id: Option<String>,
100	    user: AuthenticatedUser,
101	    perm_cache: &State<PermissionCache>,
102	    report: &State<ReportService>,
103	) -> Result<Json<KpiSummary>, AppError> {
104	    guards::require_permission(perm_cache, &user.user_id, api::REPORTS_READ).await?;
105	    guards::require_data_scope(perm_cache, &user.user_id, &user.org_id, department_id.as_deref(), project_id.as_deref(), "read").await?;
106	    let filters = ReportFilters {
107	        from_date,
108	        to_date,
109	        department_id,
110	        project_id,
111	        limit: None,
112	        offset: None,
113	    };
114	    let summary = report.kpi_summary(&user.org_id, &filters).await?;
115	    Ok(Json(summary))
116	}
117	
118	// ============================================================
119	// Export
120	// ============================================================
121	
122	#[rocket::post("/export", data = "<body>")]
123	pub async fn export_data(
124	    body: Json<ExportRequest>,
125	    user: AuthenticatedUser,
126	    perm_cache: &State<PermissionCache>,
127	    export: &State<ExportService>,
128	) -> Result<Json<ExportResult>, AppError> {
129	    guards::require_permission(perm_cache, &user.user_id, action::EXPORT_DATA).await?;
130	    guards::require_data_scope(perm_cache, &user.user_id, &user.org_id, body.department_id.as_deref(), body.project_id.as_deref(), "read").await?;
131	    let result = export.export(&user.org_id, &user.user_id, &**perm_cache, &body).await?;
132	    Ok(Json(result))
133	}
134	
135	pub fn routes() -> Vec<Route> {
136	    rocket::routes![
137	        order_volume,
138	        revenue_report,
139	        utilization_report,
140	        kpi_summary,
141	        export_data,
142	    ]
143	}
144

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# API tests: Reports & Exports
3	# Tests: order volume, revenue, utilization, KPI endpoints with date filters,
4	#        export masking defaults, unmasked export gating, unknown export type 400.
5	
6	set -euo pipefail
7	
8	BASE_URL="${BACKEND_URL:-http://localhost:8000}"
9	PASSED=0
10	FAILED=0
11	
12	check() {
13	    local name="$1"
14	    local expected="$2"
15	    local actual="$3"
16	    if [ "$actual" = "$expected" ]; then
17	        echo "  PASS  $name (got $actual)"
18	        PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
19	    else
20	        echo "  FAIL  $name (expected $expected, got $actual)"
21	        FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
22	    fi
23	}
24	
25	check_contains() {
26	    local name="$1"
27	    local needle="$2"
28	    local haystack="$3"
29	    if echo "$haystack" | grep -q "$needle"; then
30	        echo "  PASS  $name (contains '$needle')"
31	        PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
32	    else
33	        echo "  FAIL  $name (expected to contain '$needle')"
34	        echo "        Got: $haystack" | head -c 300
35	        FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
36	    fi
37	}
38	
39	check_not_contains() {
40	    local name="$1"
41	    local needle="$2"
42	    local haystack="$3"
43	    if echo "$haystack" | grep -q "$needle"; then
44	        echo "  FAIL  $name (should NOT contain '$needle')"
45	        FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
46	    else
47	        echo "  PASS  $name (does not contain '$needle')"
48	        PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
49	    fi
50	}

--
-- Business context: service route describes the geographic / logistics dimension
-- of a care plan (e.g. "client-to-clinic", "provider region", "north-metro").
-- This enables first-class route-based filtering in all reports and exports.
--
-- Non-breaking: column is nullable, default NULL.  Existing rows remain valid.
-- No existing column or index is modified.

ALTER TABLE client_plans
    ADD COLUMN service_route VARCHAR(100) DEFAULT NULL
    COMMENT 'Service route label for route-based report filtering (e.g. north-metro, client-to-clinic)'
    AFTER project_id;

-- Index for efficient report filtering when route is specified
CREATE INDEX idx_client_plans_service_route ON client_plans (service_route);
"}

456	# =============================================================
457	# Delivery export regression (service_catalog_items join fix)
458	#
459	# The export_type=deliveries query JOINs the service catalog table
460	# to resolve service_name.  A prior bug used a non-existent table
461	# alias ("service_items") causing a runtime SQL error.  These tests
462	# assert the query succeeds and the response contains the expected
463	# fields, preventing regression.
464	# =============================================================
465	
466	echo ""
467	echo "[17] Delivery export returns 200 and expected fields"
468	
469	DEL_EXPORT=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/reports/export" \
470	    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
471	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
472	    -d '{
473	        "export_type": "deliveries",
474	        "from_date": "2024-01-01",
475	        "to_date": "2024-12-31"
476	    }' 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
477	
478	DEL_STATUS=$(echo "$DEL_EXPORT" | python3 -c "
479	import sys, json
480	try:
481	    d = json.load(sys.stdin)
482	    # Must have these top-level keys
483	    assert 'rows' in d, 'missing rows'
484	    assert 'row_count' in d, 'missing row_count'
485	    assert 'masked' in d, 'missing masked'
486	    assert 'export_log_id' in d, 'missing export_log_id'
487	    print('OK')
488	except Exception as e:
489	    print(f'FAIL:{e}')
490	" 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL:parse_error")
491	check "Delivery export response shape" "OK" "$DEL_STATUS"
492	
493	# If rows exist, verify each row has the expected delivery-export fields
494	DEL_FIELDS=$(echo "$DEL_EXPORT" | python3 -c "
495	import sys, json
496	try:
497	    d = json.load(sys.stdin)
498	    rows = d.get('rows', [])
499	    if not rows:
500	        print('OK_EMPTY')
501	        sys.exit(0)
502	    expected = {'id','delivery_date','plan_id','client_name','provider_id',
503	                'service_item_id','service_name','units','mileage','status'}
504	    for i, r in enumerate(rows):
505	        missing = expected - set(r.keys())
506	        if missing:
507	            print(f'FAIL:row[{i}] missing {missing}')
508	            sys.exit(0)
509	    print('OK')
510	except Exception as e:
511	    print(f'FAIL:{e}')
512	" 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL:parse_error")
513	
514	if [ "$DEL_FIELDS" = "OK" ] || [ "$DEL_FIELDS" = "OK_EMPTY" ]; then
515	    check "Delivery export row fields present" "1" "1"
516	else
517	    check "Delivery export row fields present" "OK" "$DEL_FIELDS"
518	fi
519	
520	echo "[18] Delivery export row_count matches rows array length"
521	
522	DEL_COUNT_CHECK=$(echo "$DEL_EXPORT" | python3 -c "
523	import sys, json
524	try:
525	    d = json.load(sys.stdin)
526	    actual_len = len(d.get('rows', []))
527	    declared = d.get('row_count', -1)
528	    if actual_len == declared:
529	        print('OK')
530	    else:
531	        print(f'FAIL:row_count={declared},actual={actual_len}')
532	except Exception as e:
533	    print(f'FAIL:{e}')
534	" 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL:parse_error")
535	check "Delivery export row_count consistent" "OK" "$DEL_COUNT_CHECK"
536	
537	echo "[19] Delivery export with filters still returns 200"
538	
539	DEL_FILTER_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
540	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/reports/export" \
541	    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
542	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
543	    -d '{
544	        "export_type": "deliveries",
545	        "from_date": "2024-01-01",
546	        "to_date": "2024-12-31",
547	        "department_id": "nonexistent-dept",
548	        "project_id": "nonexistent-proj"
549	    }' 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
550	check "Delivery export with filters -> 200" "200" "$DEL_FILTER_STATUS"
551	
552	# =============================================================
553	# Summary

558	echo " Results: $PASSED passed, $FAILED failed"

Here is the complete summary of all changes:

---

## Summary

### Data Model
**`backend/migrations/20240109000000_add_service_route.sql`** (new)
- Adds nullable `service_route VARCHAR(100)` column to `client_plans` after `project_id`
- Adds index `idx_client_plans_service_route` for query performance
- Non-breaking: nullable column with NULL default, existing rows unaffected

### Backend — Domain Types
**`backend/src/domain/scoring_types.rs`**
- Added `service_route: Option<String>` to `ReportFilters` struct
- Added `service_route: Option<String>` to `ExportRequest` struct
- Both are `Option` + `Deserialize` — clients that omit the field get `None` (backward compatible)

### Backend — Report Service
**`backend/src/application/report_service.rs`**
- Renamed `validate_date_filters` to `validate_filters`; added validation that `service_route` (when provided) is non-empty after trim and <= 100 chars — returns 400 otherwise
- Added `route_clause = "AND cp.service_route = ?"` (conditional) to `order_volume`, `revenue_report`, and `utilization_report` queries
- Added bind for `service_route` in each method, positioned after date binds / before limit+offset
- `kpi_summary` accepts the parameter (for validation) but does not filter by it, matching the existing pattern where dept/project are also accepted but not applied to KPI sub-queries

### Backend — Export Service
**`backend/src/application/export_service.rs`**
- Added `service_route: Option<&str>` parameter to `export_deliveries`, `export_evaluations`, and `export_revenue`
- Added `AND cp.service_route = ?` clause and bind in each method
- Added `service_route` to the `filters_json` audit log entry

### Backend — API Routes
**`backend/src/api/reports_exports/mod.rs`**
- Added `service_route: Option<String>` query parameter to all four GET endpoints (order-volume, revenue, utilization, kpi)
- Passes it through to `ReportFilters` construction
- Existing endpoints continue to work unchanged when parameter is omitted

### Frontend
**`frontend/src/pages/reports/mod.rs`**
- Added `service_route: Signal<String>` prop to `DateRangeFilter` component with text input labeled "Service Route (optional)"
- Added `service_route` signal + URL param append to all four report tabs (KPI, Order Volume, Revenue, Utilization)
- Added `service_route` text input and conditional JSON field to `ExportTab`
- When field is left empty, no parameter is sent — existing behavior preserved

### Tests
**`API_tests/test_reports.sh`** — Added tests [20]-[25]:
- [20] All four reports without `service_route` return 200 (backward compatibility)
- [21] All four reports with `service_route=north-metro` return 200 (filter accepted)
- [22] Whitespace-only `service_route` returns 400 (validation)
- [23] Export with `service_route` returns 200
- [24] Export without `service_route` returns 200 (backward compat)
- [25] Combined `department_id + service_route` returns 200 (filter composability)

### Documentation
**`docs/scoring_and_reporting.md`** — Added `service_route` to common query parameters with description of semantics, validation rules, and column mapping
**`docs/requirements_traceability.md`** — Added service route dimension row mapping implementation, tests, and docs across all affected files

You are a senior engineer tasked with documentation correctness and static verifiability.

Issue:
- README states payment idempotency is enforced via unique constraint on `(org_id, idempotency_key)`.
- Implementation now uses 5-minute window semantics with `payment_idempotency_keys` + `ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE` logic.
- This mismatch weakens auditability.

- Make docs fully accurate and traceable to current code/migration behavior.

content.text:
1	# CareOps Service Billing & Quality Management Portal
2	
3	A locally-deployed portal for managing care service delivery, billing, and quality scoring. Built with Dioxus (Rust/WASM frontend), Rocket (Rust backend), and MySQL.
4	
5	## Tech Stack
6	
7	| Component | Technology | Version |
8	|-----------|-----------|---------|
9	| Frontend | Dioxus (Web/WASM) | 0.6 |
10	| Backend | Rocket | 0.5 |
11	| Database | MySQL | 8.0 |
12	| ORM/Migrations | sqlx | 0.7 |
13	| Auth | Argon2id + JWT | -- |
14	| Encryption | AES-256-GCM | -- |
15	| Container | Docker Compose | -- |
16	
17	## Quick Start
18	
19	```bash
20	docker compose up
21	```
22	
23	That's it. This single command:
24	1. Starts MySQL and waits for it to be healthy
25	2. Builds and starts the Rocket backend (runs migrations and seeds automatically)
26	3. Builds and serves the Dioxus frontend via nginx
27	
28	## Exposed Ports
29	
30	| Service | Port | URL |
31	|---------|------|-----|
32	| Frontend | 3000 | http://localhost:3000 |
33	| Backend API | 8000 | http://localhost:8000 |
34	| MySQL | 3306 | `mysql://careops_user:careops_pass@localhost:3306/careops` |
35	
36	## Authentication
37	
38	### Demo Accounts
39	
40	All demo accounts are seeded automatically on first startup:
41	
42	| Username | Password | Role | Permissions |
43	|----------|----------|------|-------------|
44	| `admin` | `Admin123!` | System Administrator | Full access |
45	| `ops_manager` | `OpsManager123!` | Operations Manager | Catalog, plans, delivery, billing, scoring |
46	| `billing_staff` | `Billing123!` | Billing Specialist | Billing, payments, reports |
47	| `coach` | `Coach123!` | Coach/Clinician | Plans (read), delivery (read/write) |
48	| `qa_reviewer` | `QAReview123!` | QA Reviewer | Delivery (read), scoring, reports |
49	| `auditor` | `Auditor123!` | Auditor | Audit logs, reports (read-only) |
50	
51	### Auth Flow
52	
53	1. `POST /api/auth/login` with `{"username": "...", "password": "..."}` returns JWT token + user profile
54	2. Include token as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on all subsequent requests
55	3. `POST /api/auth/logout` to invalidate session
56	4. `GET /api/auth/me` to fetch current user profile with permissions
57	
58	### Security Features
59	
60	- Password hashing: Argon2id with random salts
61	- Account lockout: 5 failed attempts -> 15-minute lock
62	- Session revocation: logout immediately invalidates the JWT
63	- Permission cache: changes take effect within 30 seconds
64	- Encryption at rest: AES-256-GCM for sensitive fields
65	- Audit logging: all auth events and security-sensitive mutations
66	- CORS origin allowlist: explicit origins only (no wildcard with credentials). Configure via `CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS` env var (comma-separated). Defaults to localhost ports in local mode.
67	- Payment idempotency: enforced atomically via database unique constraint on `(org_id, idempotency_key)`. Duplicate keys return 409 Conflict.
68	
69	See [docs/security_model.md](docs/security_model.md) for the full security architecture.
70	
71	## RBAC Summary
72	
73	Six permission categories enforce access control at every layer:
74	
75	| Category | Enforcement | Example |
76	|----------|-------------|---------|
77	| `menu.*` | Frontend sidebar visibility | `menu.dashboard`, `menu.admin` |
78	| `action.*` | Button/action visibility + backend check | `action.users.create` |
79	| `api.*` | Backend route authorization | `api.users.read`, `api.billing.write` |
80	| Data scope | Backend org/dept/project filtering | `user_data_scopes` table |
81	
82	Frontend permission checks are advisory. Backend enforcement is mandatory.
83	
84	## Observability & Resilience
85	
86	### Startup
87	
88	```bash
89	docker compose up                          # default: chaos drills off
90	CHAOS_ENABLED=true docker compose up       # arm chaos drills (Sunday 02:00 UTC only)
91	```
92	
93	### Degradation toggles
94	
95	Two in-process toggles persist in the `ops_config` DB table and can be flipped without restart:
96	
97	| Toggle key | Default | Effect when disabled |
98	|---|---|---|
99	| `exports_enabled` | `true` | Export endpoint returns 503 |
100	| `analytics_enabled` | `true` | All report endpoints return 503 |
101	
102	**Safety:** Toggle parsing is fail-closed. If a toggle value in the database is missing or malformed (not `"true"`/`"false"`), it defaults to `false` (feature disabled) and emits a warning log.
103	
104	Toggle via `POST /api/ops/flags/:key/enable|disable` (requires `api.ops.write`, System Administrator only).
105	All changes are written to `ops_events` and `audit_log`.
106	
107	### Error-rate alarm
108	
109	A background task evaluates the 10-minute sliding window error rate every 30 seconds.
110	When the rate exceeds **2%**, the alarm transitions to ALERTING and an `ops_events` row is written.
111	Visible at `GET /api/health/alerts` and `GET /api/health/metrics`.
112	
113	### Chaos drills
114	
115	Weekly, Sunday 02:00–02:15 UTC. Guarded by `CHAOS_ENABLED=true` env var (off by default).
116	Simulates 200 ms DB latency and 5% request timeouts — bounded, time-limited faults.
117	See [docs/observability_and_resilience.md](docs/observability_and_resilience.md) for full details.
118	
119	## Migrations & Seeds
120	
121	- **Migrations** run automatically when the backend starts (embedded via sqlx)
122	- **Seeds** are idempotent: checked via `_seed_history` table
123	- Seeds include: permissions, roles with permission mappings, demo org, demo users
124	- No manual database setup is required
125	
126	## Repository Structure
127	
128	```
129	repo/
130	├── docker-compose.yml
131	├── README.md
132	├── run_tests.sh
133	├── .gitignore / .dockerignore
134	│
135	├── backend/
136	│   ├── Cargo.toml
137	│   ├── Dockerfile
138	│   ├���─ migrations/
139	│   │   ├── 20240101000000_initial_schema.sql
140	│   │   └── 20240102000000_security_rbac_audit.sql
141	│   └── src/
142	│       ├── main.rs
143	│       ├── bootstrap/          # Rocket wiring, service initialization
144	│       ├── config/             # Environment configuration
145	│       ├── api/
146	│       │   ├── guards/         # JWT auth guard, permission/scope checkers
147	│       │   ├── auth/           # Login, logout, current user
148	│       │   ├── admin_org/      # Org, department, project CRUD
149	│       │   ├── users_roles_permissions/  # User, role, permission, scope CRUD
150	│       │   ├── audit_api/      # Audit log querying
151	│       │   ├── service_catalog/
152	│       │   ├── packages/
153	│       │   ├── client_plans/
154	│       │   ├── delivery_entries/
155	│       │   ├── billing/
156	│       │   ├── payments_refunds/
157	│       │   ├── scoring_reviews/
158	│       ��   ├── reports_exports/
159	│       │   ├── observability/  # Health probes (public), metrics/alerts/chaos (api.ops.read)
160	│       │   ├── ops/            # Degradation toggle controls (api.ops.read/write)
161	│       │   └── tracing_fairing.rs
162	│       ├── application/        # Service layer
163	│       │   ├── auth_service.rs
164	│       │   ├── user_service.rs
165	│       │   ├── role_service.rs
166	│       │   ├── org_service.rs
167	│       │   ├── seed_service.rs
168	│       │   ├── metrics_service.rs
169	│       │   ├── alert_engine.rs
170	│       │   ├── degradation_service.rs
171	│       │   └── chaos_service.rs
172	│       ├── domain/
173	│       │   ├── error.rs        # Typed errors -> HTTP status + JSON envelope
174	│       │   ├── auth_types.rs   # JWT claims, request/response types
175	│       │   └── auth_policy.rs  # Permission codes, role-permission matrix
176	│       └── infrastructure/
177	│           ├── database/       # Pool, migrations
178	│           ├── logging/        # Structured JSON logging
179	│           ├── encryption/     # AES-256-GCM encrypt/decrypt/mask
180	│           ├── audit/          # Immutable audit log service
181	│           └── permission_cache/  # In-process cache with version invalidation
182	│
183	├── frontend/
184	│   ├── Cargo.toml
185	│   ├── Dockerfile / Dioxus.toml / nginx.conf
186	│   ├── assets/main.css
187	│   └── src/
188	│       ├── main.rs / app.rs / router.rs
189	│       ├── layouts/app_layout.rs
190	│       ├── components/         # Sidebar (permission-aware), topbar, state components
191	│       ├── pages/
192	│       │   ├── login/          # Real auth form with demo credentials
193	│       │   ├── dashboard/
194	│       │   ├── admin/          # Org/dept/role management
195	│       │   ├── users/          # User listing and management
196	│       │   ├── audit/          # Live audit log viewer
197	│       │   └── ...             # Other domain pages
198	│       ├── services/api_client.rs  # HTTP client with auth header injection
199	│       ├── state/mod.rs            # AuthState with permission checking
200	│       └── models/mod.rs           # Shared API types
201	│
202	├── docs/
203	│   ├── architecture.md
204	│   ├── security_model.md
205	│   ├── catalog_and_delivery_workflows.md
206	│   └── requirements_traceability.md
207	│
208	├── unit_tests/backend/
209	│   ├── test_health.sh
210	│   └── test_password_hashing.sh
211	│
212	└── API_tests/
213	    ├── test_smoke.sh
214	    ├── test_auth.sh
215	    └── test_catalog_delivery.sh
216	```
217

content.text:
1	# CareOps Security Model
2	
3	## 1. Authentication
4	
5	### Flow
6	
7	1. User submits `username` + `password` to `POST /api/auth/login`
8	2. Backend looks up the user and verifies credentials against Argon2id hash
9	3. On success: creates a session record, issues a JWT containing `user_id`, `session_id`, `org_id`
10	4. Client stores the JWT and includes it as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on subsequent requests
11	5. On logout (`POST /api/auth/logout`): session is marked as revoked in the database
12	
13	### Password Storage
14	
15	- Algorithm: Argon2id (via the `argon2` crate with default parameters)
16	- Salts: randomly generated per password (SaltString from OsRng)
17	- Plaintext passwords are never stored, logged, or returned in API responses
18	
19	### Session Management
20	
21	- Sessions are stored in the `sessions` table with a SHA-256 hash of the JWT token
22	- Each session has an `expires_at` timestamp and optional `revoked_at`
23	- Token validation checks: JWT signature + expiration + session not revoked
24	- Token revocation is immediate on logout
25	
26	### Account Lockout
27	
28	- Failed login attempts are tracked in `user_credentials.failed_attempts`
29	- After 5 consecutive failures, account is locked for 15 minutes (`locked_until`)
30	- Successful login resets the failure counter
31	
32	### Implementation Files
33	
34	| Component | File |
35	|-----------|------|
36	| Password hashing | `backend/src/application/auth_service.rs` (hash_password, verify_password) |
37	| JWT issuance/validation | `backend/src/application/auth_service.rs` (login, validate_token) |
38	| Session storage | `backend/migrations/20240102000000_security_rbac_audit.sql` (sessions table) |
39	| Auth request guard | `backend/src/api/guards/mod.rs` (AuthenticatedUser) |
40	| Claims type | `backend/src/domain/auth_types.rs` (JwtClaims) |
41	
42	---
43	
44	## 2. RBAC Model
45	
46	### Architecture
47	
48	The RBAC system uses three layers of permission control:
49	
50	```
51	User -> [User Roles] -> Role -> [Role Permissions] -> Permission
52	```
53	
54	Permissions are categorized by enforcement point:
55	
56	| Category | Purpose | Example |
57	|----------|---------|---------|
58	| `menu` | Frontend sidebar/navigation visibility | `menu.dashboard`, `menu.admin` |
59	| `action` | Button/action visibility and backend enforcement | `action.users.create`, `action.billing.generate` |
60	| `api` | API endpoint authorization | `api.users.read`, `api.billing.write` |
61	| `data` | Data-scope access rules | (enforced via data_scopes table) |
62	
63	### Default Roles
64	
65	| Role | Description | Key Permissions |
66	|------|-------------|-----------------|
67	| **System Administrator** | Full access to all system features | All permissions |
68	| **Operations Manager** | Operational oversight across delivery and billing | Catalog, plans, delivery, billing, scoring management |
69	| **Billing Specialist** | Billing and payment processing | Billing, payments, plan/delivery read |
70	| **Coach/Clinician** | Service delivery logging | Plans read, delivery read/write |
71	| **QA Reviewer** | Quality scoring and review | Delivery read, scoring read/write, reports |
72	| **Auditor** | Read-only audit and compliance | Audit log read, reports read/export |
73	
74	### Permission Enforcement Points
75	
76	1. **Frontend navigation**: Sidebar links only render for permitted menu items (`components/sidebar.rs`)
77	2. **Frontend actions**: Buttons/actions conditionally render based on permission set (`state/mod.rs: has_permission()`)
78	3. **Backend API guard**: `AuthenticatedUser` request guard rejects unauthenticated requests with 401
79	4. **Backend route-level**: `guards::require_permission()` checks permission codes before handler logic
80	5. **Backend object-level**: `guards::require_data_scope()` checks org/department/project access
81	
82	Frontend visibility is advisory only. Backend enforcement is mandatory and independent.
83	
84	### Implementation Files
85	
86	| Component | File |
87	|-----------|------|
88	| Permission definitions | `backend/src/domain/auth_policy.rs` |
89	| Role-permission matrix | `backend/src/domain/auth_policy.rs` (default_role_permissions) |
90	| Permission cache | `backend/src/infrastructure/permission_cache/mod.rs` |
91	| Route-level guards | `backend/src/api/guards/mod.rs` |
92	| Frontend permission check | `frontend/src/state/mod.rs` (AuthState::has_permission) |
93	| Frontend sidebar | `frontend/src/components/sidebar.rs` |
94	
95	---
96	
97	## 3. Data-Scope Model
98	
99	### Scope Hierarchy
100	
101	```
102	Organization
103	  └── Department
104	       └── Project
105	```
106	
107	Each user has explicit data-scope grants stored in `user_data_scopes`:
108	
109	| Field | Purpose |
110	|-------|---------|
111	| `org_id` | Required: which organization's data the user can access |
112	| `department_id` | Optional: restricts to a specific department |
113	| `project_id` | Optional: restricts to a specific project |
114	| `access_level` | `read`, `write`, or `admin` |
115	
116	### Scope Resolution Rules
117	
118	1. An **org-level** scope (department_id=NULL) grants access to all departments and projects within that org
119	2. A **department-level** scope grants access to all projects within that department
120	3. A **project-level** scope grants access only to that specific project
121	4. Access level is hierarchical: `admin` > `write` > `read`
122	5. **System Administrator** role bypasses all scope checks
123	
124	### Enforcement
125	
126	Data-scope is enforced at the API level via `guards::require_data_scope()`:
127	
128	```rust
129	guards::require_data_scope(
130	    perm_cache, &user.user_id,
131	    &org_id, department_id.as_deref(), project_id.as_deref(),
132	    "write" // required access level
133	).await?;
134	```
135	
136	This is called before any data-modifying operation to ensure the user has appropriate scope access.
137	
138	### Implementation Files
139	
140	| Component | File |
141	|-----------|------|
142	| Scope table | `backend/migrations/20240102000000_security_rbac_audit.sql` (user_data_scopes) |
143	| Scope checking | `backend/src/infrastructure/permission_cache/mod.rs` (check_data_scope) |
144	| Guard helper | `backend/src/api/guards/mod.rs` (require_data_scope) |
145	| Scope CRUD | `backend/src/application/user_service.rs` (assign_scope, revoke_scope) |
146	
147	---
148	
149	## 4. Permission Cache / Versioning
150	
151	### Design
152	
153	The permission cache prevents hitting the database on every API request while ensuring permission changes take effect within 30 seconds.
154	
155	```
156	┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐
157	│  API Request  │────▶│  Permission     │────▶│  permission_    │
158	│               │     │  Cache (in-     │     │  version table  │
159	│  check perm   │     │  process,       │     │  (single row)   │
160	│               │     │  RwLock)        │     │                 │
161	└──────────────┘     └──────────────────┘     └─────────────────┘
162	```
163	
164	### Mechanism
165	
166	1. Every TTL interval (max 30 seconds), the cache reads the global `version` from `permission_version`
167	2. If the version changed since last check, the entire cache is cleared
168	3. Individual user entries are loaded from DB on cache miss
169	4. Any permission/role/scope change increments the global version counter
170	5. Thread safety via `parking_lot::RwLock` for low-contention reads
171	
172	### Version Invalidation
173	
174	These operations increment the permission version (trigger cache refresh):
175	- Role assignment/revocation (`user_service.assign_role/revoke_role`)
176	- Permission grant/revocation (`role_service.assign_permission/revoke_permission`)
177	- Data-scope grant/revocation (`user_service.assign_scope/revoke_scope`)
178	
179	### Timing Guarantee
180	
181	- Cache TTL is capped at 30 seconds in code: `Duration::from_secs(ttl_seconds.min(30))`
182	- Worst case: a permission change is visible to all requests within 30 seconds
183	- The change is immediately visible to the user whose cache entry was invalidated
184	
185	### Implementation Files
186	
187	| Component | File |
188	|-----------|------|
189	| Cache implementation | `backend/src/infrastructure/permission_cache/mod.rs` |
190	| Version table | `backend/migrations/20240102000000_security_rbac_audit.sql` (permission_version) |
191	| Cache invalidation calls | `backend/src/application/user_service.rs`, `role_service.rs` |
192	
193	---
194	
195	## 5. Encryption at Rest
196	
197	### Approach
198	
199	Sensitive fields are encrypted using AES-256-GCM before storage in MySQL. The encryption key is derived from environment-provided key material using SHA-256.
200	
201	### Encrypted Format
202	
203	```
204	base64( 12-byte-nonce || ciphertext || authentication-tag )
205	```
206	
207	Each encryption operation generates a random 12-byte nonce, ensuring identical plaintext produces different ciphertext.
208	
209	### Key Management
210	
211	- Key material is provided via the `ENCRYPTION_KEY` environment variable
212	- SHA-256 is used to derive a 256-bit key from the key material
213	- The docker-compose.yml includes a dev-safe default key (must be changed for production)
214	- The `EncryptionService` struct redacts the key in Debug output
215	
216	### Encrypted Fields
217	
218	| Table | Field | Purpose |
219	|-------|-------|---------|
220	| `client_plans` | `client_identifier_enc` | Encrypted client identifier |
221	| `client_plans` | `notes_enc` | Encrypted sensitive notes |
222	| `delivery_entries` | `notes` | Encrypted when containing sensitive content |
223	| `quality_scores` | `notes` | Encrypted when containing sensitive content |
224	
225	### Masking
226	
227	The `EncryptionService::mask()` function provides safe display of sensitive values (e.g., `"12*****89"`). Used in API responses and log output to prevent sensitive data leakage.
228	
229	### Implementation Files
230	
231	| Component | File |
232	|-----------|------|
233	| Encryption service | `backend/src/infrastructure/encryption/mod.rs` |
234	| Encryption tests | `backend/src/infrastructure/encryption/mod.rs` (tests module) |
235	| Key configuration | `backend/src/config/mod.rs`, `docker-compose.yml` |
236	
237	---
238	
239	## 6. Audit Logging
240	
241	### Strategy
242	
243	All security-sensitive actions are logged to an immutable `audit_logs` table. The audit service is fire-and-forget: audit write failures are logged but never block the calling operation.
244	
245	### Logged Events
246	
247	| Category | Actions |
248	|----------|---------|
249	| **Authentication** | `auth.login.success`, `auth.login.failed`, `auth.logout` |
250	| **User Management** | `user.created`, `user.updated`, `user.deactivated`, `user.password.changed` |
251	| **Role Changes** | `role.created`, `role.assigned`, `role.revoked` |
252	| **Permission Changes** | `permission.granted`, `permission.revoked` |
253	| **Scope Changes** | `scope.granted`, `scope.revoked` |
254	| **Organization** | `org.created`, `org.updated`, `department.created`, `project.created` |
255	| **Configuration** | `config.changed` |
256	
257	### Audit Record Structure
258	
259	```json
260	{
261	  "id": "uuid",
262	  "timestamp": "2024-01-01T00:00:00",
263	  "user_id": "actor-uuid",
264	  "action": "user.created",
265	  "resource_type": "user",
266	  "resource_id": "target-uuid",
267	  "org_id": "org-uuid",
268	  "details": {"username": "newuser", "email": "..."},
269	  "ip_address": "192.168.1.1",
270	  "trace_id": "request-trace-id"
271	}
272	```
273	
274	### What Is NOT Logged
275	
276	- Passwords (neither plaintext nor hashed)
277	- Full client identifiers (masked if included)
278	- Decrypted sensitive notes
279	- Encryption keys or secrets
280	
281	### Querying
282	
283	Audit logs are queryable via `GET /api/audit/` with filters:
284	- `user_id`: filter by acting user
285	- `action`: filter by action prefix (e.g., `auth.` for all auth events)
286	- `resource_type`: filter by resource type
287	- Pagination via `limit` and `offset`
288	
289	Access requires the `api.audit.read` permission (Auditor and System Administrator roles).
290	
291	### Implementation Files
292	
293	| Component | File |
294	|-----------|------|
295	| Audit service | `backend/src/infrastructure/audit/mod.rs` |
296	| Action constants | `backend/src/infrastructure/audit/mod.rs` (actions module) |
297	| Audit API | `backend/src/api/audit_api/mod.rs` |
298	| Audit table | `backend/migrations/20240102000000_security_rbac_audit.sql` |
299	
300	---
301	
302	## 7. Security Configuration
303	
304	### Environment Variables
305	
306	| Variable | Purpose | Default (Dev) |
307	|----------|---------|---------------|
308	| `JWT_SECRET` | JWT signing key | `careops-dev-jwt-secret-...` |
309	| `ENCRYPTION_KEY` | AES-256-GCM key material | `careops-dev-encryption-key-...` |
310	| `SESSION_TTL_HOURS` | JWT/session expiration | `24` |
311	| `DATABASE_URL` | MySQL connection string | `mysql://careops_user:careops_pass@db:3306/careops` |
312	
313	### Production Checklist
314	
315	- [ ] Change `JWT_SECRET` to a cryptographically random 64+ character string
316	- [ ] Change `ENCRYPTION_KEY` to a cryptographically random 64+ character string
317	- [ ] Set `SESSION_TTL_HOURS` to appropriate value (e.g., 8 for business hours)
318	- [ ] Change MySQL passwords in docker-compose.yml
319	- [ ] Enable TLS termination in front of the backend
320	- [ ] Review and restrict CORS allowed origins
321	- [ ] Disable demo user seeds or change default passwords
322

content.text:
1	# Billing & Financial Controls
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	The billing engine converts verified service delivery records into auditable financial transactions. The flow is strictly additive: every real money movement is recorded as an immutable `fund_transaction` entry. No billing record is ever deleted or overwritten — corrections are new records that reference the original.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## 1. Billing Lifecycle
10	
11	```
12	Delivery Entry (verified)
13	        │
14	        ▼
15	   Charge Generated  ──── Charge Adjustment (optional, additive)
16	        │
17	        ▼
18	   Invoice (draft) ──── Line Items (one per charge, immutable snapshot)
19	        │
20	        ▼
21	   Invoice (issued)
22	        │
23	        ├──── Recorded Payment ──── Fund Transaction (credit, immutable)
24	        │           │
25	        │           ▼
26	        │     Invoice → paid / partially_paid
27	        │
28	        └──── Recorded Refund ──── Fund Transaction (debit, immutable)
29	                    │
30	                    ▼
31	              Net paid recalculated
32	```
33	
34	### States
35	
36	**Charge statuses**
37	
38	| Status | Meaning |
39	|--------|---------|
40	| `pending` | Generated, not yet adjusted or invoiced |
41	| `adjusted` | Has one or more charge adjustments |
42	| `invoiced` | Included in an invoice; no further adjustments allowed |
43	| `voided` | Excluded from billing (manual void) |
44	
45	**Invoice statuses**
46	
47	| Status | Meaning | Allowed next states |
48	|--------|---------|---------------------|
49	| `draft` | Generated, not yet sent | `issued`, `voided` |
50	| `issued` | Sent to payer | `paid`, `partially_paid`, `voided` |
51	| `partially_paid` | Partial payment received | `paid`, `voided` |
52	| `paid` | Full net payment received | _(terminal)_ |
53	| `voided` | Cancelled | _(terminal)_ |
54	
55	---
56	
57	## 2. Charge Generation
58	
59	**Endpoint:** `POST /api/billing/charges/generate`
60	
61	**Permission required:** `action.billing.generate`
62	
63	The service iterates all verified delivery entries for the given plan that do not already have a charge record (idempotent). For each entry it looks up the matching `package_rule_definitions` row and applies the appropriate computation:
64	
65	| Rule Type | Computation |
66	|-----------|------------|
67	| `per_visit` | `gross = rate` |
68	| `hourly` | `gross = units × rate` |
69	| `tiered` | Brackets applied in order; units fill lower brackets first |
70	
71	**Tiered billing example:**
72	```json
73	[
74	  {"up_to": 4.0, "rate": 50.00},
75	  {"up_to": 8.0, "rate": 45.00},
76	  {"up_to": null, "rate": 40.00}
77	]
78	```
79	For 10 hours: `4×$50 + 4×$45 + 2×$40 = $460`
80	
81	Entries without a matching package rule are skipped (counted in `skipped` response field).
82	
83	---
84	
85	## 3. Charge Adjustments
86	
87	**Endpoint:** `POST /api/billing/charges/:id/adjustments`
88	
89	**Rules:**
90	- Amount cannot be zero
91	- Reason text is required
92	- Only allowed on charges in `pending` or `adjusted` status (not `invoiced` or `voided`)
93	- Positive amount increases the charge; negative decreases it
94	- Adjustment records are immutable once created
95	- The parent charge's `adjustment_total` and `net_amount` are recomputed after each adjustment
96	
97	---
98	
99	## 4. Invoice Generation
100	
101	**Endpoint:** `POST /api/billing/invoices/generate`
102	
103	**Permission required:** `action.billing.generate`
104	
105	**Process:**
106	1. Collects all charges in `pending` or `adjusted` status, linked to delivery entries whose `delivery_date` falls within `billing_period_start` to `billing_period_end`
107	2. Computes `subtotal` (sum of gross amounts), `total_adjustments`, and `total_amount` (sum of net amounts)
108	3. Generates an `invoice_number` of the form `INV-{YYYYMM}-{plan_suffix}`
109	4. Creates immutable `invoice_line_items` rows (one per charge — snapshot of amounts at time of invoicing)
110	5. Marks all included charges as `invoiced` and links them to the new invoice
111	
112	Returns 400 if no pending charges exist for the plan in the given period.
113	
114	---
115	
116	## 5. Idempotency Model
117	
118	**Endpoint:** `POST /api/payments/`
119	
120	Every payment request must include an `idempotency_key` (caller-supplied, free-form string). The system enforces a **5-minute duplicate rejection window**:
121	
122	- If the same `idempotency_key` was used for the same organization within the last 5 minutes → **409 Conflict**
123	- After 5 minutes, the same key is accepted as a new (retry) payment — this handles network retries without double-charging
124	
125	**Best practice for callers:**
126	- Use a UUID generated at the time the user clicks "Record Payment"
127	- Retain the key for retry in case of network failure
128	
129	The key is not globally unique — it is scoped per organization and per 5-minute window. Two organizations may use the same key without conflict.
130	
131	**Implementation:** A dedicated `payment_idempotency_keys` table tracks active keys with a `PRIMARY KEY (org_id, idempotency_key)`. The application uses `INSERT … ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE` with a 5-minute `created_at` check, which is both race-safe (no SELECT-then-INSERT gap) and time-windowed (keys older than 5 minutes are atomically refreshed). MySQL's affected-rows semantics distinguish fresh/expired keys (proceed) from active keys (reject 409).
132	
133	---
134	
135	## 6. Refund Controls
136	
137	**Endpoint:** `POST /api/payments/refunds`
138	
139	**Permission required:** `action.payments.refund`
140	
141	**Mandatory reason code:** Every refund requires a `reason_code` from the `refund_reason_codes` table.
142	
143	| Code | Label |
144	|------|-------|
145	| `BILLING_ERROR` | Billing Error |
146	| `SERVICE_NOT_REND` | Service Not Rendered |
147	| `DUPLICATE_CHARGE` | Duplicate Charge |
148	| `CONTRACT_CHANGE` | Contract/Rate Change |
149	| `CLIENT_REQUEST` | Client-Requested Adjustment |
150	| `PARTIAL_SERVICE` | Partial Service Delivered |
151	| `QUALITY_ISSUE` | Quality Issue |
152	| `INSURANCE_ADJ` | Insurance/Payer Adjustment |
153	| `OTHER` | Other (requires detailed notes) |
154	
155	**Net-paid cap enforcement:**
156	
157	```
158	net_paid = sum(recorded_payments) − sum(recorded_refunds_so_far)
159	refund.amount must be ≤ net_paid
160	```
161	
162	If `refund.amount > net_paid`, the request is rejected with **400 Bad Request** and a message specifying the exact cap.
163	
164	This cap is enforced atomically before inserting the `fund_transaction` entry.
165	
166	---
167	
168	## 7. Immutable Fund Transaction Ledger
169	
170	The `fund_transactions` table is the authoritative financial ledger. Every real money movement — payment received or refund issued — creates exactly one row with:
171	
172	| Field | Value |
173	|-------|-------|
174	| `transaction_type` | `payment` or `refund` |
175	| `direction` | `credit` (payment in) or `debit` (refund out) |
176	| `amount` | Always positive |
177	| `reference_id` | Links to `recorded_payments.id` or `recorded_refunds.id` |
178	| `actor_id` | User who initiated the transaction |
179	| `created_at` | Immutable timestamp |
180	
181	**There are no UPDATE or DELETE endpoints for fund_transactions.** Corrections are made by recording additional payments or refunds that reference the original invoice, leaving the full trail intact.
182	
183	**Endpoint:** `GET /api/payments/transactions?invoice_id=&limit=&offset=`
184	**Permission required:** `api.payments.read`
185	
186	---
187	
188	## 8. Reconciliation
189	
190	**Endpoint:** `POST /api/payments/reconciliation`
191	
192	**Permission required:** `api.billing.read`
193	
194	A reconciliation run computes a point-in-time financial summary for a given period:
195	
196	| Field | Computation |
197	|-------|------------|
198	| `total_charges` | Sum of `gross_amount` for charges with delivery dates in period (non-voided) |
199	| `total_adjustments` | Sum of `adjustment_total` for same charges |
200	| `total_invoiced` | Sum of `total_amount` for invoices created in period (non-voided) |
201	| `total_paid` | Sum of payments with `payment_date` in period |
202	| `total_refunded` | Sum of refunds with `refund_date` in period |
203	| `net_collected` | `total_paid − total_refunded` |
204	| `outstanding_balance` | `total_invoiced − net_collected` |
205	| `pending_charge_count` | Charges in `pending` or `adjusted` status |
206	| `invoiced_charge_count` | Charges in `invoiced` status |
207	| `paid_invoice_count` | Invoices in `paid` status |
208	
209	Reconciliation runs are immutable snapshots — they cannot be updated or deleted. New runs can be generated at any time.
210	
211	---
212	
213	## 9. API Reference
214	
215	### Billing (charges + invoices)
216	
217	| Method | Path | Permission | Purpose |
218	|--------|------|-----------|---------|
219	| POST | `/api/billing/charges/generate` | `action.billing.generate` | Generate charges from verified deliveries |
220	| GET | `/api/billing/charges` | `api.billing.read` | List charges (plan_id, status filters) |
221	| GET | `/api/billing/charges/:id` | `api.billing.read` | Charge detail with adjustments |
222	| POST | `/api/billing/charges/:id/adjustments` | `action.billing.generate` | Post charge adjustment |
223	| POST | `/api/billing/invoices/generate` | `action.billing.generate` | Generate invoice from pending charges |
224	| GET | `/api/billing/invoices` | `api.billing.read` | List invoices (plan_id, status filters) |
225	| GET | `/api/billing/invoices/:id` | `api.billing.read` | Invoice detail with line items |
226	| PUT | `/api/billing/invoices/:id/status` | `action.billing.approve` | Advance invoice status |
227	
228	### Payments & Refunds
229	
230	| Method | Path | Permission | Purpose |
231	|--------|------|-----------|---------|
232	| GET | `/api/payments/reason-codes` | `api.payments.read` | List refund reason codes |
233	| POST | `/api/payments/` | `action.payments.record` | Record payment (idempotency enforced) |
234	| GET | `/api/payments/` | `api.payments.read` | List payments |
235	| GET | `/api/payments/:id` | `api.payments.read` | Get payment detail |
236	| POST | `/api/payments/refunds` | `action.payments.refund` | Record refund (cap enforced, reason required) |
237	| GET | `/api/payments/refunds` | `api.payments.read` | List refunds |
238	| GET | `/api/payments/refunds/:id` | `api.payments.read` | Get refund detail |
239	| GET | `/api/payments/transactions` | `api.payments.read` | List fund transactions (immutable ledger) |
240	| POST | `/api/payments/reconciliation` | `api.billing.read` | Generate reconciliation run |
241	| GET | `/api/payments/reconciliation` | `api.billing.read` | List reconciliation runs |
242	| GET | `/api/payments/reconciliation/:id` | `api.billing.read` | Get reconciliation run detail |
243	
244	---
245	
246	## 10. Role Access Matrix
247	
248	| Role | Can Do | Cannot Do |
249	|------|--------|-----------|
250	| System Administrator | All billing operations | — |
251	| Operations Manager | Generate charges and invoices, approve invoices | Record payments, process refunds |
252	| Billing Specialist | Record payments, process refunds, generate charges and invoices, approve invoices, run reconciliation | — |
253	| Coach/Clinician | None (read denied) | All billing operations |
254	| QA Reviewer | None (read denied) | All billing operations |
255	| Auditor | Read billing/payment data (via `api.billing.read` — not granted by default; admin can assign) | All write operations |
256	
257	---
258	
259	## 11. Audit Trail
260	
261	Every state-changing billing operation emits an audit entry:
262	
263	| Action | Trigger |
264	|--------|---------|
265	| `billing.charge.generated` | Charge creation |
266	| `billing.charge.adjusted` | Adjustment posted |
267	| `billing.invoice.generated` | Invoice created |
268	| `billing.invoice.status_updated` | Invoice status changed |
269	| `billing.payment.recorded` | Payment recorded |
270	| `billing.refund.recorded` | Refund recorded |
271	| `billing.reconciliation.generated` | Reconciliation run created |
272	
273	All entries are queryable via `GET /api/audit/` with `resource_type=charge`, `invoice`, `payment`, `refund`, or `reconciliation_run`.
274

251	| Status validation unit tests | `billing_types::tests` | `cargo test --lib` | Billing doc |
252	
253	### Recorded Payments
254	
255	| Requirement | Impl | Test | Doc |
256	|------------|------|------|-----|
257	| Record payment with idempotency key | `application/payment_service.rs` (record_payment) | `test_billing.sh` [15] | Billing doc S5 |
258	| 5-minute duplicate rejection window | `payment_service.rs` (INSERT ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE into `payment_idempotency_keys`; rows_affected=0 → 409), `migrations/20240108000000_restore_idempotency_window.sql` | `test_billing.sh` [16, 26] | Billing doc S5 |
259	| Different key accepted | `payment_service.rs` | `test_billing.sh` [17] | Billing doc S5 |
260	| Fund transaction created on payment | `fund_transactions` (credit, immutable) | `test_billing.sh` [22] | Billing doc S7 |
261	| Invoice status auto-updated after payment | `payment_service.rs` (reconcile_invoice_payment_status) | Code inspection | Billing doc |
262	
263	### Recorded Refunds
264	
265	| Requirement | Impl | Test | Doc |
266	|------------|------|------|-----|

content.text:
1	-- Migration: Restore 5-minute idempotency window for payments
2	--
3	-- The previous migration (20240107) replaced the time-windowed idempotency
4	-- check with a permanent UNIQUE constraint on (org_id, idempotency_key).
5	-- This broke the documented 5-minute window behavior: callers should be able
6	-- to reuse the same key after 5 minutes (network retry scenario).
7	--
8	-- This migration introduces a dedicated `payment_idempotency_keys` table
9	-- with a PRIMARY KEY on (org_id, idempotency_key).  The application uses
10	-- INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE with a 5-minute time check, which is
11	-- both race-safe (no SELECT-then-INSERT gap) and time-windowed.
12	--
13	-- The UNIQUE constraint on recorded_payments is dropped because the same
14	-- key can now appear in multiple payment records (after window expiry).
15	
16	-- 1. Create the idempotency tracking table
17	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS payment_idempotency_keys (
18	    org_id CHAR(36) NOT NULL,
19	    idempotency_key VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
20	    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
21	    PRIMARY KEY (org_id, idempotency_key)
22	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
23	
24	-- 2. Seed from existing payments so in-flight keys are still protected
25	INSERT IGNORE INTO payment_idempotency_keys (org_id, idempotency_key, created_at)
26	SELECT org_id, idempotency_key, created_at FROM recorded_payments;
27	
28	-- 3. Drop the permanent unique constraint from recorded_payments
29	ALTER TABLE recorded_payments DROP INDEX uq_payments_org_idempotency_key;
30	
31	-- 4. Restore a non-unique index for lookup performance
32	CREATE INDEX idx_payment_idempotency ON recorded_payments (org_id, idempotency_key, created_at);
33

content.text:
116	## 5. Idempotency Model
117	
118	**Endpoint:** `POST /api/payments/`
119	
120	Every payment request must include an `idempotency_key` (caller-supplied, free-form string). The system enforces a **5-minute duplicate rejection window**:
121	
122	- If the same `idempotency_key` was used for the same organization within the last 5 minutes → **409 Conflict**
123	- After 5 minutes, the same key is accepted as a new (retry) payment — this handles network retries without double-charging
124	
125	**Best practice for callers:**
126	- Use a UUID generated at the time the user clicks "Record Payment"
127	- Retain the key for retry in case of network failure
128	
129	The key is not globally unique — it is scoped per organization and per 5-minute window. Two organizations may use the same key without conflict.
130	
131	**Implementation:** A dedicated `payment_idempotency_keys` table tracks active keys with a `PRIMARY KEY (org_id, idempotency_key)`. The application uses `INSERT … ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE` with a 5-minute `created_at` check, which is both race-safe (no SELECT-then-INSERT gap) and time-windowed (keys older than 5 minutes are atomically refreshed). MySQL's affected-rows semantics distinguish fresh/expired keys (proceed) from active keys (reject 409).
132	
133	---

- The `payment_idempotency_keys` table
- The `INSERT ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE` mechanism
- The `affected-rows` semantics
